segments.s661-g161: This clause places the subject جهان (the world) first, followed by a prepositional/relational phrase introduced by زیر meaning 'subject to / under the control of' and then the two nouns آیین and فرهنگ joined by و. ماست is the copula (poetic order: predicate followed by ما 'our' then ست 'is'), so literally 'the world is under our law and culture.'
segments.s661-g162: سپهر روان is a nominal phrase: سپهر 'the sky/heavens' qualified by روان 'moving/flowing' (an adjectival/epistemic sense). جوشن جنگ is a genitive-like (protective) phrase 'the cuirass/armor of (our) war'; ماست again is the copula placed after the predicate, giving 'the flowing heavens are the armor of our war.'
segments.s661-g163: بدو is a dative pronoun 'to him' placed before the verb; گفت here functions as 'said/spoke' (past), with شاهان پیشین 'the earlier/former kings' as the grammatical subject and دراز 'long' (adverbially) qualifying the speech. Word order is somewhat inverted (dative → verb → subject → adverb).
segments.s661-g164: سخن 'speech/words' is the object/topic and خواستند is the past-tense verb 'they desired/wanted.' آشکارا 'openly' and راز 'secret' form a paired adverbial (joined by و) describing the manner of speaking; the clause says they wished for speech both public and private.
segments.s661-g165: شما را is a dative/ topical phrase 'for you' (را marking the person as the beneficiary/target here). سخن کمتر 'less speech' and داد بیش 'more justice' are contrasted (کمتر ... و ... بیش). The clause states a preference or observation about the addressees: fewer words and more justice.
segments.s661-g166: فزون داری is 'you have more/greater abundance' (فزون modifying داری 'you possess'). از ... پیش is a comparative 'than the renowned ones before'—نامداران پیش 'the famous/renowned predecessors.' The clause completes the comparison begun in the previous segment.
segments.s661-g167: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase 'thus gave answer' (چنین 'thus/so', داد 'gave' as a verb, پاسخ 'reply/answer'). The following که introduces the content of that reply; هر شهریار begins a free relative 'whichever king.'
segments.s661-g168: که باشد ورا یار پروردگار is the continuation of the relative clause: هر شهریار که باشد 'whichever king he may be' (باشد in subjunctive/optative sense after هر), ورا = 'for him/to him', and یار پروردگار 'the Lord's ally/companion' is the predicate (the copula is implied). The whole means the Lord is his ally regardless of which king he is.
segments.s661-g193: زمانی: a temporal adverb 'at any time/ever'. نباشد: negative present/subjunctive form of بودن, here impersonal 'there is not / there never is' (classical usage); none of the listed senses match exactly so I mark it as -1. ب + دان together function as بهِ + آن 'about/at that' (classical fusion), where دان here behaves like 'to consider/that' (see note on دان). شادمان: predicate adjective 'joyful, rejoicing'. The clause means 'there is no time at which he is joyful about that.'
segments.s661-g194: به اندیشه: 'with thought' or 'in thought' (به marks the state/contents). دارد: present tense 'has/possesses' (here stative: 'is possessed of'). همیشه: 'always'. روان: 'soul/spirit' used as the subject that 'has thought'. Word order is slightly inverted (state phrase before verb): 'his spirit always has thought / his spirit is always thoughtful.'
segments.s661-g195: چنین داد پاسخ: 'thus gave an answer' — داد is the past tense 'gave' (he replied). که introduces the content of the reply ('that'). اندیشه نیست: 'there is no thought' or 'no intention exists' (نیست = 'is not'). The whole clause is a report of the reply: 'he replied that there is no thought/intent.'
segments.s661-g196: دل شاه: 'the king's heart' (possessive noun phrase). با چرخ گردان: 'with the revolving sky/heavens' — با = 'with'. چرخ گردان is a classical epithet for the heavens/fate (چرخ = sky/heaven, گردان = 'revolving'). یکی‌ست: predicate 'is one / is identical' (archaic copula form). The line says the king's heart is aligned with the turning heavens.
segments.s661-g197: بترسم که: 'I fear that...' — بترسم is a present-subjunctive/optative form meaning 'I am afraid' introducing a subordinate clause. هرکو ستایش کند: 'whoever praises (it/him) does' — هرکو = 'whoever', ستایش = 'praise/worship', کند is the present subjunctive/optative 'may do/does' used in subordinate clauses. The clause expresses the speaker's worry about those who will praise.
segments.s661-g198: مگر: 'unless' / 'except that' (introduces the condition that would prevent the fear). بیم ما را: 'our fear' or 'our safety/concern' with را marking the definite object. نیایش کند: 'he prays / offers supplication' (نیایش کردن). The sequence means 'I fear that whoever praises (him), unless he prays for our safety' — i.e. the speaker fears praise unaccompanied by prayer on their behalf.
segments.s661-g199: ستایش نشاید فزون: 'praise ought not to be excessive' — نشاید is an archaic impersonal/modal 'it is not fitting/should not'. فزون = 'excessive/more than proper'. زآنک هست: 'because there is / since there is' — zآنک gives the reason that follows. The clause states a moral/practical injunction about limits to praise.
segments.s661-g200: نجوییم: first-person-plural hortative/negative 'let us not seek' or 'we should not inquire' (نجوییم = ن + جوییم). راز دل زیردست: 'the secret of the vassal/underling's heart' — راز = secret, دل = heart, زیردست = 'subordinate/underling'. Together: 'let us not pry into the secret of the (king's) subordinate's heart.'
segments.s661-g153: هرآنگه که is a temporal conjunction meaning “whenever/when.” سال اندر آید is a simple present/habitual use of آید (“comes”) with اندر “in/into,” literally “the year comes in.” به شست is a prepositional/adverbial phrase (به + شست); poetically it marks the moment of the year’s passing (literally “when the year has been ‘washed’/completed”) and so modifies the whole temporal clause.
segments.s661-g154: به پیش = “in the presence of / before.” مدارا is a noun meaning “forbearance, tolerance.” بباید is the modal auxiliary meaning “one must / it is necessary” (present tense, used impersonally), and نشست here is the verb/infinitive “to sit” (i.e. “one must sit / one should adopt a sitting, composed attitude”). The whole phrase prescribes adopting a tolerant demeanour before others.
segments.s661-g155: سپاس is a noun/exclamation “thanks / praise.” از marks the source/recipient here (lit. “to/of”), so سپاس از جهاندار پروردگار = “praise to the world-holder, the Sustainer.” جهاندار is an epithet “holder/sovereign of the world,” and پروردگار is a title “Lord / Nurturer.”
segments.s661-g156: کزویست is a fused poetic form meaning “which/that is from him” (کز + وی + است). نیک و بد روزگار = “the good and the bad of the times/fate”; نیک “good,” و “and,” بد “bad,” روزگار “times/fate.” Together the clause says that good and bad alike originate from that Lord.
segments.s661-g157: که introduces an explanatory/reason clause (“for / because”). روز جوانی is a temporal/genitive phrase “the days/time of youth.” هنر داشتیم is past-tense possession: هنر “skill/virtue” and داشتیم “we had” (past), stating that in youth they possessed skill.
segments.s661-g158: بد و نیک را places the two items (bad and good) as the definite objects (را marks the definite/direct object); the order (bad before good) is a stylistic choice. خوار نگذاشتیم is a causative-negative past: خوار “despised / of low worth” and نگذاشتیم “we did not allow / we did not let” (past), so the whole means “we did not let (them) be despised / we did not treat them with contempt.”
segments.s661-g159: کنون “now” sets the time. روز پیری = “the days/time of old age.” به دانندگی uses به + an abstract noun (دانندگی “knowledge/knowing”) to indicate possession or condition—“with knowledge” or “in wisdom” in this stage of life.
segments.s661-g160: This is a triadic list introduced by repeated به: به رای “with counsel/opinion/plan,” به گنج “with treasure/wealth,” و فشانندگی “and liberality/effusiveness.” رای here means counsel/plan, گنج means treasure/wealth, and فشانندگی describes a disposition to scatter/spend (i.e. generosity or lavish giving). The repeated به frames each item as a quality/possession of the speaker in old age.
segments.s661-g9: 'یکی' here means 'someone/a certain person' and serves as the subject. 'دست برداشته' is a participial construction (past participle + noun) meaning 'having raised a hand' or simply 'raising a hand' — a descriptive clause attached to 'یکی'. 'به آسمان' is a directional phrase 'toward/to the sky/heaven' modifying the action.
segments.s661-g10: 'همی‌خواهد' is an older/poetic present tense form (prefix همی- marks continuous or habitual action) meaning 'is asking/imploring' or 'desires'. 'از کردگار جهان' uses the preposition 'از' (from) plus 'کردگار جهان' = 'the Creator of the world' as the source addressed; together it means 'is asking of the Creator of the world'.
segments.s661-g11: 'نیابد' is the negative present form (from یابیدن) meaning 'does not find/attain' — here 'cannot obtain'. 'به خواهش' uses 'به' in the sense 'by/through' (means or manner) + 'خواهش' (a plea/request). 'همه آرزو' 'all wishes' is the object of 'نیابد' (what he cannot attain by pleading).
segments.s661-g12: 'دو چشمش' is 'his two eyes' with the possessive clitic -ش. 'پر از آب' literally 'full of water' = figuratively 'full of tears'. 'و پر چینش رو' repeats the adjective 'پر' with 'چینش' (folding/wrinkling) and 'رو' (face) to say 'and his face full of folds/wrinkles'. Word order places possessive noun + predicate adjectives after it.
segments.s661-g13: 'به موبد' is an indirect-object prepositional phrase 'to the mobed (the priest)'. 'چنین گفت' = 'thus said'. The order places the indirect object first, then the verb phrase, and finally the subject 'پیروز شاه' ('the victorious king').
segments.s661-g14: The clause introduced by 'که' reports the content: 'خواهش ز یزدان' = 'requests from God' (ز is poetic 'from'). 'به اندازه' = 'within measure/moderation', and 'خواه' is a verb meaning 'should ask/wish' (here jussive/instructional: one ought to ask within bounds).
segments.s661-g15: 'کزان' = 'from which' (ablative/relative), introducing the source: 'from such a desire'. 'دل پر از خون شود' uses 'پر از' = 'full of' and 'شود' (شد) as 'becomes' — 'the heart becomes full of blood' (figuratively: inflamed, agitated, or stained by bloodshed).
segments.s661-g16: 'که' here gives the explanation/cause 'for' or 'that': 'خواهد' = 'it desires/wishes' (third-person imperfect). The second 'که' introduces the subordinate clause 'ز اندازه بیرون شود' = 'to go/become outside of measure' where 'بیرون شود' is subjunctive-like 'become outside' — overall: 'for it desires to exceed the proper measure.'
segments.s661-g265: چو here introduces a simile ('like, as'); رادی is used as a substantive/adjective 'a generous (one)'. که is the relative particle 'who/that' introducing the clause. پاداش precedes the verb as its object ('reward'), and نجست is the past of جستن used here with the sense 'sought' — so the whole clause means 'like a generous (man) who sought no reward' (the negative sense is implied by context in the poem). Word order is topical object + verb, typical of Persian poetry.
segments.s661-g266: ببخشید is a past 3rd-person form here meaning 'he gave/bestowed' (classical/poetic use that looks like modern imperative/polite form). و simply links two actions. تاریکی is the direct object 'darkness'; از دل is a prepositional phrase 'from the heart'; بشست is the past 'he washed' — together 'he washed the darkness from his heart.' The two verbs are coordinated (he gave, and he washed...).
segments.s661-g267: سه دیگر is a numeral phrase 'three others'. چو introduces a comparison 'like'. کوشایی is a noun/adjective 'one who strives; striving', and ایزدی modifies it as 'divine/godly' — 'three others like a divine striver.' The order (numeral + دیگر + چو + noun phrase) is poetic and topicalizes the count.
segments.s661-g268: که begins a relative clause 'who/that'. از جان پاک آید uses آید (present/simple form 'comes') with از + جان پاک to mean 'comes from a pure soul'; the verb آید governs both جان پاک and the following nominal بخردی, so the clause means 'who come from a pure soul and from prudence/wisdom.' و simply coordinates the two sources. بخردی is a noun 'prudence/wisdom' and is linked as a second source without repeating از (a classical economy of expression).
segments.s661-g269: بپرسید is past 3rd-person 'he asked'. در دل هراس is a prepositional phrase 'with fear in (his) heart' (دل = heart, هراس = fear). از چه بیش is an interrogative fragment 'about/why what more' or 'why more' — بیش here functions as 'more/else' and the clause is elliptical, continuing into the reported speech that follows.
segments.s661-g270: بدو گفت is the light verb construction 'he said to him' (بدو = to him; گفت = said). کز is the relative/complimentary particle 'that/from which' introducing the content of what was said. رنج و کردار خویش lists two nouns 'suffering' and 'deeds/behavior' followed by the enclitic ش (خویش → خویش+ش) meaning 'his/own', so the phrase means 'from his own suffering and deeds.' The clause functions as the reported answer to the fearful question.
segments.s661-g271: بپرسید again is 'he asked'. بخشش is the noun 'giving/donation' (here 'which act of giving'). کدامست is an archaic fused form of 'کدام است' meaning 'which is it?'. به is the preposition 'by/for/through' here introducing the beneficiary/measure that the next clause will specify ('by which'). The phrase is an interrogative set-up leading into the explanatory clause.
segments.s661-g272: که introduces the explanatory/result clause 'that/which'. بخشنده is used as 'the giver' (agent of giving). گردد is the classical 3rd-person present/subjunctive of گشت/گردیدن 'becomes' (here 'becomes/turns into'). سرافراز and مه are predicative adjectives coordinated by و: سرافراز 'honored/uplifted' and مه in this poetic sense means 'great/mighty' (an archaic adjectival use rather than 'moon'). The clause therefore asks which gift makes the giver 'become honored and great.'
segments.s661-g129: شما را places 'you' as a marked direct object (را object marker after the pronoun), so the phrase functions as 'praise of you' or 'your praise'. ستایش is a noun 'praise' functioning as the subject/predicate; فزونست is an older/poetic fused form meaning 'is greater' or 'surpasses' (third-person singular). ازان ('from that' / 'than that') introduces the comparison 'than that'.
segments.s661-g130: خروش و نیایش is a coordination 'outcry/clamor and prayer' with و linking the two nouns. فزون‌ست repeats the predicative 'is greater/abundant' applied to the coordinated nouns, and ازان again marks the comparative 'than that'. Word order (noun + predicate + comparative) is typical of classical/poetic Persian.
segments.s661-g131: چنین داد پاسخ is literally 'thus gave an answer' — داد is the finite verb 'gave' and پاسخ is its direct object; together they mean 'answered thus'. که is the subordinator 'that' introducing the content clause. یزدان پاک is the noun phrase 'the pure God' (یزدان = God, پاک = 'pure' as an adjective after the noun in classical order).
segments.s661-g132: پرستنده را uses را to mark the worshipper as the object affected by the action. سر برآرد is a compact classical/periphrastic verb: سر 'head' + برآرد (برآورد/برآرد) 'raises' — here third person singular, present/future sense 'will raise/raises'. ز خاک 'from the dust' is a prepositional phrase (ز = 'from'; خاک = 'dust/earth'), completing the verb phrase.
segments.s661-g133: فلک را sets 'the heavens' as the object (را). گزارنده is a participial/predicative form ('one who causes to pass / conveyor' in the lexicon) used here as a predicate complement meaning 'the one associated with/power over the heavens'; او is the clitic/independent pronoun 'him/he', and کند is the classical present/future-tense verb 'makes/does' (third-person singular). The structure is object + predicate-nominal + pronoun + verb: 'He makes him [the one over] the heavens.'
segments.s661-g134: جهان را is the marked object 'the world'; همه modifies بنده as 'all' or 'entire' (i.e., 'the whole world'). بنده ('servant') is used predicatively and او is the pronoun 'his/he' (referring back to the person made exalted); کند again is the verb 'makes'. The clause reads 'he makes the whole world his servant.'
segments.s661-g135: گر is the conditional 'if'. این بنده 'this servant' is the conditional subject; آن را is the thing whose value is in question ('that' + object marker). نداند is the classical negative present/subjunctive third person 'does not know' (or 'should not know' depending on context). بها means 'value/worth' here — so the clause means 'if this servant does not know that worth.'
segments.s661-g136: مبادا is a wish/negation particle 'may it not be; lest' expressing a negative wish or curse. ز درد و ز سختی is a coordinated prepositional phrase 'from pain and from hardship' (ز = 'from'). رها here is a predicative adjective/verb 'free, released'; together the clause is a wish that he not be freed 'from pain and hardship' (i.e. may he remain in affliction if he fails to recognize the worth).
segments.s661-g201: بدو گفت is a short clause meaning "he said to him": بدو is the prepositional pronoun 'to him/unto him' placed before the verb, and گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (archaic narrative style). شادی ز فرزند چیست is a copular question: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of', فرزند is the noun 'child', and چی + ست (چیست) forms the interrogative 'what is'. Word order is Topic (شادی ز فرزند) + question copula (چیست).
segments.s661-g202: همان آرزوها is a noun phrase 'those very wishes' (همان = 'the same/that'), followed by the genitive/agentive phrase ز پیوند 'from/of the bond' (ز = poetic 'from/of', پیوند = 'bond/link, here = union/marriage'). The clause ends with the copular question چیست 'what is it?'. The order is determiner + noun + prepositional phrase + interrogative copula.
segments.s661-g203: چنین داد پاسخ is a classical order meaning 'thus gave an answer' (چنین 'thus/so' modifying داد 'gave' and/or پاسخ 'answer'). که introduces the content clause 'that...'; هرکو is an archaic fused form of هر که 'whoever/anyone who', and جهان here refers to 'this life/the world'. The clause begins a subordinate explanatory sentence.
segments.s661-g204: به فرزند ماند is 'remains to/for the child' (به = 'to/for', فرزند = 'child', ماند = past/perfect stem 'remains/was left'). نگردد نهان is negated passive/optative phrasing: ن-گردد (negative + گردد 'become/turn') + نهان 'hidden' = 'does not become hidden' or 'will not remain concealed'. Word order places the verb before its adjectival complement.
segments.s661-g205: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when'. چو فرزند باشد = 'when there is a child' (باشد is the present/subjunctive copula used here in third person). بیابد مزه is 'finds/experiences pleasure' (بیابد = 'finds/attains/experiences', مزه = abstract 'pleasure/delight'). The sequence is temporal clause + resulting verb phrase.
segments.s661-g206: ز بهر مزه is a prepositional phrase 'for the sake of (that) pleasure' (ز = 'because of/for', بهر = 'for/for the sake of', مزه = 'pleasure'). دور گردد بزه uses گرد (here گردد = 'becomes/is turned') with دور 'far/removed' and بزه 'fault/wrongdoing': literal order Verb (گردد) after adjunct (دور) with the noun بزه as logical subject/object — together 'fault becomes distant' or 'wrongdoing is driven away.'
segments.s661-g207: وگر is the conditional 'but if/if however'. بگذرد is the present/subjunctive of گذشت 'passes' (here 'if it passes' or 'if it dies/passes away' depending on context). کم بود درد اوی is a verbless/predicative structure meaning 'his pain was/ is small' (کم = 'small/less', بود = past/copula 'was' used in narrative registers, درد = 'pain/sorrow', اوی = archaic clitic 'his/he').
segments.s661-g208: که here introduces an explanatory/subordinate clause and functions like 'for/because' ('that'/'because'). فرزند بیند رخ زرد اوی = 'he sees his child's pale face' (فرزند = 'child', بیند = third-person present 'he sees', رخ = 'face', زرد = 'pale/wan', اوی = 'his/he'). The clause explains or gives the reason/scene related to the preceding statement.
segments.s661-g49: 'به هفتم' is an ordinal/introductory phrase meaning 'as to the seventh' or 'the seventh [point]'. 'که' is the complementizer 'that' introducing the clause. 'از نیک و بد در جهان' means 'concerning/of good and bad in the world' — here 'از' has the sense 'concerning' and the list 'نیک و بد' are coordinated direct objects of 'که'.
segments.s661-g50: 'سخن‌ها' (the speeches/words) is the subject. 'بروبر' is an adverb meaning 'openly/face-to-face' (manifestly) modifying the predicate. 'نماند' is the present third-person negative of 'ماندن' = 'does not remain / is not left'. 'نهان' is the predicate adjective 'hidden'; together 'بروبر نماند نهان' = 'are not left hidden, are openly revealed.'
segments.s661-g51: 'چو' here means 'when' or 'if' (introducing a condition). 'فر' (farr) is the special notion of royal/divine glory and 'خرد' is 'wisdom'; both are objects of the verb 'دارد' ('he has/possesses'). The clause continues the enumeration with 'و دین و بخت' — 'and religion and fortune' (additional qualities possessed).
segments.s661-g52: The predication is 'سزوار تاج است' — 'he is worthy of the crown.' 'سزوار' is a predicative adjective 'worthy' placed before the noun for emphasis; 'است' is the copula 'is'. 'و زیبای تخت' adds 'and (of) the beautiful throne' — 'زیبای تخت' = 'the beautiful throne'. Together: 'he is worthy of the crown and the fair throne.'
segments.s661-g53: 'به هشتم' introduces the eighth point. 'که' introduces the content clause. 'دشمن بداند' uses 'بداند' (third-person present/subjunctive of 'دانستن') here with an optative/relative sense 'that the enemy know / may know' rather than a simple factual present. 'ز دوست' (poetic 'ز' = 'از') means 'from the friend' — indicating the source of knowledge.
segments.s661-g54: 'بی‌آزاری' (literally 'without harming') functions as the noun 'harmlessness / inoffensiveness'. 'از شهریاران' here means 'among/of kings/rulers' (indicating the social sphere where the quality is considered). 'نکوست' is an archaic predicative form meaning 'is good / is fitting'. So the clause states that harmlessness, among rulers, is good.
segments.s661-g55: 'نماند' is the present negative 'does not remain / will not be left'. 'پس از مرگ او' is the temporal phrase 'after his death' (here 'پس' + 'از' = 'after'). 'نام زشت' = 'an ugly/vile name' but contextually 'a bad reputation' — 'نام' is used as 'reputation' and 'زشت' 'vile/bad'. The whole segment: 'After his death no bad reputation remains.'
segments.s661-g56: 'بیابد' (from یافتن) is used as a present/subjunctive/optative meaning 'he finds / he attains' (future-oriented here). 'به فرجام' = 'at/to the end / in the outcome'. 'خرم' is 'joyful/blissful' qualifying 'بهشت' which is 'paradise'. Together: 'in the end he attains a joyful paradise.'
segments.s661-g233: بپرسد is the narrative/question verb (classical present-subjunctive form used as past narrative) meaning "he asked." چه introduces the question "what." کارست is the contracted poetic form of کار است ("is a deed/task"), so the phrase کارست برتر ز مرگ literally means "what deed is superior to death" with ز + مرگ (from/death) used comparatively.
segments.s661-g234: اگر باشد is a conditional clause meaning "if there is/if it exists" (باشـد here = "be"). این را uses the demonstrative plus the definite-object/ topicalizing marker را ("this [one]" as object/topic). چه سازیم is a rhetorical question with سازیم (1st-person-plural present/subjunctive/optative) meaning "what shall we make/do (with it)." برگ here denotes a leaf/page; the whole line asks what to do with that leaf if something superior to death exists.
segments.s661-g235: چنین داد پاسخ is an idiomatic sequence meaning "thus gave an answer/answered so" (چنین = "thus," داد = "gave," پاسخ = "answer"). کزین (کز + این) means "from this/ hence from this," and تیره خاک is a poetic noun phrase "dark dust/earth," describing what must be passed.
segments.s661-g236: اگر بگذری is a conditional with بگذری (2nd-person-singular subjunctive/present) meaning "if you pass/are able to pass." یافتی is the 2nd-person past form of یافتن used here in classical narration to indicate the ensuing result ("you will find"). جان پاک = "a pure soul/ life."
segments.s661-g237: هرآنکس که introduces a relative clause "whoever/anyone who." در بیم و اندوه زیست places the action "lived" inside a state: در = "in," بیم = "fear," اندوه = "sorrow," and زیست is the past/ narrative form "lived."
segments.s661-g238: بران is a compact prepositional/locative particle here meaning "for that / concerning that." زندگی زار is a noun + adjective phrase "wretched life" (زار = "wretched"), and باید گریست is an impersonal/modal construction meaning "one must weep/it is necessary to weep."
segments.s661-g239: بپرسد again introduces "he asked." کزین دو گران‌تر کدام is a compressed comparative question: کزین دو = "of these two," گران‌تر = "more heavy/ burdensome," and کدام = "which (one)." Word order is classical/poetic and omits some copula elements.
segments.s661-g240: کزوییم is a fused prepositional/pronominal form (کز + وی + ایم) meaning roughly "from/of which one of us." پر is the preposition/adjective "full (of)," so پر درد = "full of pain/sorrow." و links the descriptors, and ناشادکام (literary) means "ill-fated/unsuccessful or unhappy," describing the condition of that person.
segments.s661-g113: بپرسید is an imperative/poetic past form meaning 'ask' or 'inquired'; here it opens a question. کز is the classical fused relative 'که + از' meaning 'who/that from' (i.e. 'who from'). تخت شاهنشهان is a genitive phrase 'the throne of the shahanshahs' (treating شاهنشهان as the possessive noun following تخت). Word order places the relative phrase after the verb 'بپرسید' as the thing to ask about.
segments.s661-g114: بکردی is a classical/perhaps archaic past-form of کردن with the preverb ب-; in epic Persian such a form functions as the main past verb 'made/did' in the relative clause. همه شهریار جهان is the object phrase 'every/all the rulers of the world' with همه ('all/every') modifying شهریار ('ruler/king') and جهان ('world') as a further specifier.
segments.s661-g115: کنون is an adverb 'now' setting the time. نامشان is the noun 'name' with the 3rd-person plural possessive suffix شان 'their' ('their name(s)/reputation'). بیش is an adverb 'more/further' modifying the following verbal phrase. یاد آوریم is composed of یاد ('remembrance') + آوریم (آورد + first-person-plural suffix) and means 'we bring to mind / we recall' — آوریم here is a volitional/presentive 'let us/we bring' construction in literary style.
segments.s661-g116: بیاد is a prepositional/adverbial phrase 'into memory' or 'in remembrance'. از marks the source 'from'. جگر سرد is a poetic phrase 'cold liver/heart' (جگر being the seat of feeling); سرد here is literal/figurative 'cold'. باد is the optative particle 'may' (classical poetic usage) that with آوریم ('may we bring') expresses a wish; آوریم repeats the first-person-plural verb 'we bring'. Overall: 'may we bring (them) to mind from a cold heart.'
segments.s661-g117: چنین داد پاسخ is an inverted expression 'gave such an answer' (چنین = 'thus/so', داد = 'gave', پاسخ = 'answer'); classical Persian allows the object 'پاسخ' before the verb. که introduces the reported content. در دل نبود is a past-clause 'was not in the heart' (در = 'in', دل = 'heart', نبود = past 'was not/existed not'), meaning 'he did not feel it / it was not in his heart.'
segments.s661-g118: The second that-clause (که ...) gives the content of the answer: آن رسم را (the custom, with را marking it as definite object) خود is an intensifier meaning 'itself' or 'indeed', and نباید ستود is an impersonal negative modal 'ought not to be praised' (نباید = 'should/must not', followed by the verbal root/stem ستود 'to praise'). Word order places the object before the modal phrase for emphasis.
segments.s661-g119: به شمشیر و داد این جهان داشتن is an infinitival/nominal phrase 'to have/hold this world by the sword and (by) justice' — به indicates instrument or means ('by/with'), شمشیر 'sword', و 'and', داد 'justice' (here the abstract noun), این جهان 'this world', and داشتن is the verbal noun/infinitive 'to have/possess'. The construction describes the manner of ruling/possessing the world.
segments.s661-g120: چنین رفتن و خوار بگذاشتن: چنین 'thus/so', رفتن is the infinitive/gerund 'to go' (in epic usage can also imply 'to perish/leave'), و 'and', خوار is an adjective 'despised/humiliated' used adverbially or as predicate, and بگذاشتن is the causative/compound verb 'to leave/abandon' (ب- prefix + گذاشتن). Together: 'to go thus and to leave (one) humiliated.' The object of بگذاشتن is omitted (understood from context).
segments.s661-g185: چنین داد پاسخ is a poetic inversion of the usual order such as 'چنین پاسخ داد' — داد is the past tense 'gave' and پاسخ is its direct object ('gave an answer'). که introduces the content clause. تن بی‌زمان is a noun plus postpositive adjective: 'تن' (body/person) qualified by 'بی‌زمان' ('without time' = untimely/premature).
segments.s661-g186: که begins the subordinate clause 'that...'. 'پیش آید' functions as a verbal phrase 'comes/happens earlier' (پیش as adverb + آید 'comes/happens' in simple tense). از marks the cause/source ('from'), and گردش آسمان is a genitive phrase 'the rotation/revolution of the heavens' that supplies the cause.
segments.s661-g187: بجایست is an impersonal/expletive expression 'it is fitting/appropriate' (introducing the condition). دارو نیاید به کار is literal 'medicine does not come to use' — نیاید is the negative present/optative form of آمدن used here to mean 'is of no avail', and به کار is an adverbial phrase 'to/for use'.
segments.s661-g188: گردش روزگار is the subject phrase 'the turning/rotation of time/fate'. نگه داردش is a verbal phrase where نگه (keeping/watch) precedes the verb داردش ('does/has it' with enclitic object -ش = 'him/it'); together 'keeps him in its hold'. Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic meter (object pronoun attached to the verb).
segments.s661-g189: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. هنگامه رفتن is a nominal phrase 'the moment/clamor of going' where رفتن is a verbal noun (gerund) 'of going/departure'. آمد فراز is a poetic sequence: آمد (past 'came/arrived') with فراز ('on high'/'up'), meaning 'rose/arrived on high' (i.e. the time arrived).
segments.s661-g190: زمانه is 'the age/fortune/the course of time'. نگردد is the negative present/future form of گردیدن 'turn' (here 'will not be turned/back'), used impersonally of fate. به پرهیز باز is an adverbial construction 'back/by avoidance' — i.e., 'it will not turn back simply through avoidance/caution.'
segments.s661-g191: بپرسید is a past narrative 'he asked' (classical/formal past). چندان is an adverb 'to that extent/so much' modifying the verb phrase. ستایش کنند is a subordinate verbal clause meaning 'they praise' (کنند is the subjunctive/imperfective form used after a past reporting verb to introduce the content of the question).
segments.s661-g192: جهان‌آفرین را is the direct object with the explicit object marker را ('the Creator' as object). نیایش کنند is the verb phrase 'they perform worship/prayer' (نیایش = prayer; کنند = do/will do), parallel to the previous clause; object precedes the verb for emphasis.
segments.s661-g97: بپرسید is an imperative/polite plural form of پرسیدن here meaning 'ask'. کس را is the object ('someone') marked by را. از + the verbal noun آموختن ('learning') means 'about / concerning learning' — so the whole segment = 'ask someone about learning.'
segments.s661-g98: ستایش ندیدم is a simple past negative with ندیدم ('I did not see') and ستایش ('praise') as object: 'I saw no praise.' The و links a parallel nominal phrase, افروختن here is a verbal noun meaning 'the kindling or arousal (of zeal)' so the clause means 'nor (did I see) any kindling.'
segments.s661-g99: که introduces a relative / content clause ('which/that'). نیزش = نیز + -ش (a demonstrative/relational suffix) = 'it too / that also'. ز دانا 'from the wise', and بباید + شنید gives an impersonal obligation 'ought to be heard' (literally 'must be heard').
segments.s661-g100: نگویم is a first-person negated present ('I do not say' / 'I will not say'). کسی کو is an archaic relative phrase where کو functions like 'who' (lit. 'someone who'), and به‌جایی رسید 'reached a place/position' — together: 'I will not say (this of) anyone who has reached a post.'
segments.s661-g101: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed order meaning 'he gave such an answer' (چنین 'thus', داد 'gave', پاسخ 'answer'). The following که begins the quoted explanation: از گنج سیر = 'sated/full with treasure' (سیر as adjective meaning 'sated').
segments.s661-g102: This is the remainder of the quoted reply. که آید is a rhetorical 'what comes / what happens', مگر here means 'except / other than', خاکش 'his dust' (خاک + possessive -ش), آرد (آورد) 'brings' and به زیر 'under / beneath (the earth)'. The clause reads as a rhetorical question: 'what else comes but that his dust is borne beneath the earth?'
segments.s661-g103: در دانش 'in knowledge/learning' sets the domain; از گنج is the standard of comparison ('than treasure'). نامی‌ترست is an archaic comparative form meaning 'is more famed' or 'is more celebrated' — so: 'Fame in learning is greater than in treasure.'
segments.s661-g104: همان ('that same') refers back to the fame of knowledge; نزد دانا literally 'in the sight / estimation of the wise' (نزد = 'with / in the presence of / in the judgment of'), and گرامی‌ترست is 'is more valued / honored'. So: 'That is more esteemed among the wise.'
segments.s661-g105: سخن ماند از ما همی یادگار = literal word order places predicate before possession: 'سخن ماند' (our words remain) + 'از ما' ('of/from us') + 'همی' (still/ever) + 'یادگار' (a memorial). 'ماند' is perfective/present-stative here meaning 'remain/ endure.' The whole phrase means 'let these words remain as a remembrance of me.'
segments.s661-g106: تو با گنج دانش برابر مدار = 'تو' is the (poetic) address 'you'; 'با گنج دانش' is a prepositional phrase 'with the treasure of knowledge'; 'برابر مدار' is an imperative/jussive prohibition in this context meaning 'do not make equal' or 'do not equate.' Together: 'Do not equate (them) with the treasure of knowledge.'
segments.s661-g107: بپرسید دانا شود مرد پیر = 'بپرسید' (they asked) introduces an indirect question. The clause that follows uses inverted order: 'دانا شود' (becomes wise) precedes the subject 'مرد پیر' (the old man). 'شود' is subjunctive/present of 'to become' used here as 'becomes.'
segments.s661-g108: گر آموزشی باشد و یاد‌گیر = conditional clause introduced by 'گر' ('if'); 'آموزشی باشد' literally 'there be instruction' with 'آموزشی' functioning as 'instruction/teaching' and 'باشد' as subjunctive/present 'there is/may be'; 'و یاد‌گیر' is coordinated and means '(and) that he be a learner' or 'and he learns' — parallel conditions for becoming wise.
segments.s661-g109: چنین داد پاسخ که دانای پیر = 'چنین داد پاسخ' is a formulaic phrase 'he gave such an answer that...' ('چنین' thus/so, 'داد' gave, 'پاسخ' answer). The follow-up 'که دانای پیر' introduces the reported content: 'that the wise elder...'. Word order places the descriptor before what follows.
segments.s661-g110: ز دانش جوانی بود ناگزیر = 'ز' (from/with respect to) marks the domain 'دانش' (knowledge); 'جوانی بود' literally 'was youth/young' i.e. 'was young in knowledge'; 'ناگزیر' is an adverb/adjectival predicate meaning 'inevitably/necessarily.' The clause therefore states that, in terms of knowledge, the old sage was inevitably (still) 'young' — the phrasing implies a paradox about age vs. learning.
segments.s661-g111: بر ابله جوانی گزینی رواست = here 'جوانی گزینی' is a verbal-noun/infinitive 'to choose youth'; 'بر ابله' indicates 'over/against a fool' (i.e., 'instead of a fool' or 'rather than a fool'); 'رواست' = 'it is fitting/permissible.' The construction '... گزینی رواست' states a judgement that choosing youth in preference to a fool is proper.
segments.s661-g112: که بی‌گور او خاک او بی‌نواست = 'که' gives the reason ('for/that'); 'بی‌گور' ('without a grave') describes the fool; 'او' is the pronoun 'he/his'; 'خاک او' = 'his earth' (i.e. his burial place or dust); 'بی‌نواست' is a predicative adjective 'is wretched/destitute.' The sense: because a fool without a grave is left with a pitiable earth (i.e. is ignoble/unmourned).
segments.s661-g73: This is a nominal phrase where the ordinal adjective 'نخستین' (first) modifies the verbal noun phrase 'سخن گفتن' (speaking words). 'سودمند' is a predicate adjective qualifying that verbal noun (i.e. ‘speaking that is useful’). Word order is typical Persian: modifier + verbal noun + adjective; 'گفتن' is the infinitive/verbal noun form.
segments.s661-g74: This clause uses an elided subject; 'خوش‌آواز' is an epithet (‘melodious’), and 'خواند' here is best read as a past transitive 'called' (they called him so). The clitic pronoun 'ورا' = 'him/her/it' is the object of 'خواند'. 'بی‌گزند' is a predicate adjective following the object, meaning 'unharmed' or 'left without harm.'
segments.s661-g75: 'دگر' (= another/other) introduces a second item. 'آنک' is a connector/relative 'that/which' introducing the clause. The sequence 'پیمان سخن خواستن' is a noun + noun + infinitive construction: 'پیمان سخن' = 'a pledge (concerning) speech' and 'خواستن' is the verb 'to ask/request' — so the phrase means 'to request a pledge of speech' (object precedes the verb, common in Persian nominalized lists).
segments.s661-g76: 'سخنگوی' is the agent noun 'speaker'; 'و' is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; 'بینا' is an adjective 'discerning/clear‑sighted' modifying 'دل' (heart). 'آراستن' is the infinitive 'to adorn/prepare' and attaches as the verbal goal of the preceding notion (i.e. to make the speaker ready and to adorn the discerning heart). The verb is in infinitive form because this whole line lists proper actions/qualities.
segments.s661-g77: The initial 'که' introduces a purposive/result clause ('so that'); 'چندان' is an adverb 'to such an extent/so much'; 'سراید' is the present/subjunctive form of 'سرودن/سراییدن' meaning 'compose/sing' (here purposive: 'may compose'); the second 'که' introduces the result clause 'that it come to use' ('آید' = 'come/happen' + 'به کار' = 'to use/serve a purpose'). The double 'که ... که ...' pattern expresses extent → result/purpose.
segments.s661-g78: 'وزو' here is an archaic fused form meaning roughly 'from that/therefrom' (not the modern literal sense 'ablution'); because that exact sense isn't in the provided list it is marked as unmatched. 'ماند' (remains) is used in the sense 'endures/lasts'; 'اندر' = 'in/within'; 'جهان' = 'world'; 'یادگار' = 'memorial/memento' — the clause says that from that work a memorial endures in the world.
segments.s661-g79: This nominal group names 'three others' (سه دیگر) described as 'سخنگوی' (speakers). 'هنگام‌جوی' is a compound adjective 'time‑seeking' / 'opportunistic' qualifying those speakers. It enumerates a third class of persons: three opportunistic speakers.
segments.s661-g80: 'بماند' is the present/subjunctive or modal-present 'remain/let remain' (the prefix ب- is the classical present marker), here indicating continued existence ('they remain'). 'همه‌ساله' = 'every year/annual'; 'بر' = 'on/upon'; 'آب' = 'water'; 'روی' = 'surface/face' — together 'بر آب روی' = 'on the surface of the water.'
segments.s661-g145: زبون is a predicate noun meaning 'cowardly/abject' here; بود is the past copula 'was'; در جنگ من is a locative prepositional phrase 'in my battle' with من marking possession ('my'). Word order is predicate — copula — locative, a common poetic ordering.
segments.s661-g146: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; گوپال من is a possessed noun phrase 'my cowherd' (گوپال used as the common noun 'cowherd' here); دید is the simple past 'saw'; و links the two objects, and اورنگ من is another possessed noun phrase 'my throne' (اورنگ = throne/seat of power). The clause gives the circumstance under which the previous predicate holds.
segments.s661-g147: بپرسید is a past-tense verb 'he asked' (third person); در جنگ خاور is a locative phrase 'in the battle of the east' (خاور = 'east'); بدی here is a noun 'harm/evil' (object of the implied question). The whole segment sets up the subject and topic of the question.
segments.s661-g148: چنان is an intensifier 'such'; تیز modifies چنگ as 'sharp' and چنگ is a metaphorical 'claw/strike'; و is the conjunction 'and'; دلاور is used adjectivally/nominatively 'valiant' (referring to a force or combatant) and بدی repeats the noun 'harm/evil'. The phrase lists the qualities of the harm asked about.
segments.s661-g149: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'; با باختر literally 'with the West' here means 'against the West' or 'in dealings with the West'; ساختی is second-person past 'you made/constructed'; ساز جنگ is a compound 'instrument/means of war' (ساز used as 'device/means' + جنگ 'war'). The clause describes the action performed by 'you'.
segments.s661-g150: شکیبایی is the noun 'patience'; آراستی is a past-tense verb 'you adorned/arranged' (continuing the second-person narration); با درنگ is an adverbial phrase 'with deliberation/with delay' (با = 'with', درنگ = 'delay/deliberation'). Together they state that patience was fashioned or practiced with careful deliberation.
segments.s661-g151: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrasing 'he gave such an answer' (چنین = 'thus', داد = 'gave', پاسخ = 'answer'); که introduces the content of the answer ('that'); مرد جوان is the subject of the subordinate clause 'a young man' (مرد = man, جوان = young, used together as 'young man').
segments.s661-g152: نیندیشد is the negative past-tense verb 'did not think/ponder' (third person); از introduces the object 'of' or 'about'; رنج و درد روان is a coordinated noun phrase 'the sufferings and pains of the soul' (رنج = suffering, درد = pain, روان = soul). The clause states the content of the answer: that the young man does not consider such spiritual suffering.
segments.s661-g281: Three predicates are coordinated by و (and). Each predicate carries the old copula -ست (is): بزرگست and برترست are adjectival predications 'is great' / 'is superior', while داننده is an agent-noun 'knower' used as a predicate; the order is predicate(+copula) — و — predicate, a common classical Persian listing of attributes.
segments.s661-g282: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause ('who/for that'); بر here marks superiority/over (above) the following noun phrase. داوران جهان is a genitive-like compound 'judges of the world', and داورست is a nominal-predicate meaning 'is (the) judge/arbiter' (i.e., he is the judge above the world's judges).
segments.s661-g283: بد آیین مشو is an imperative prohibition: مشو (do not become) modifies the noun phrase بد آیین ('bad custom/manners'). دور باش از پسند is a second imperative cluster: باش is the imperative 'be', modified by دور 'far' and governed by از 'from' + پسند 'approval/what pleases' — 'keep away from approval.'
segments.s661-g284: مبین functions as a predicative adjective or verblike predicate 'clear/manifest' (here: 'it is clear' / 'know clearly'). ایچ is the emphatic negative 'nothing/not at all' that negates the following phrase; ازو 'from it' and سود و ناسودمند 'profit and unprofitable' state that there is neither profit nor loss in it.
segments.s661-g285: بد و نیک از او دان is an exhortation: دان (from دانستن) here functions as 'know!' (imperative/verb of cognition) and is followed by the statement 'بد و نیک از او' ('good and bad are from him'). کش is the productive/agentive element attached to form a compound (here read as part of the classical phrase), while انباز is an archaic/uncertain noun; the clause ends with نیست 'is not'.
segments.s661-g286: به کاریش is a prepositional phrase 'in/for his work' (کار + possessive suffix ایش). فرجام و آغاز نیست states that there is no end (فرجام) and no beginning (آغاز) to that work — a simple existential negation using نیست.
segments.s661-g287: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. گوید is the 3rd-person verbal form 'he says' (here rendered in the source senses list irregularly), and بباش is the quoted imperative 'Be!' (the divine command). آنچ 'that which' is the relative pronoun; گوید (again 'he says') and بدست 'by/through the hand' indicate that whatever he commands comes to pass by his power.
segments.s661-g288: همو 'the same one' is the subject; بود (past 'was') combined with تا 'as long as/until' forms a temporal clause 'he was as long as there was (being)'; the parallel تا هست هست uses تا + present هست and a final copula هست for emphasis — a poetic way to assert his eternal presence ('he was, and is while there is being').
segments.s661-g57: بپرسیدش (bapersīdash) is a narrative past/imperfect form meaning “they asked him” (imperative reading is possible in other contexts, but here the following line gives his reply so past narrative fits). از introduces the content of the question (= “about/concerning”). داد here means “justice” (a noun) and is conjoined by و to خردک منش, where خردک is a diminutive/poetic form related to خرد (“wisdom, sense”) and منش is “character” — together “wisdom/character.”
segments.s661-g58: ز is a poetic preposition equivalent to از meaning “about” or “concerning.” نیکی = “goodness” (what he has done well). وز here is a fused sequence (و + ز) functioning like “and about / and concerning”; the provided lexical senses list doesn’t include that combined usage, so the sense is not one of the listed options. مردم = “people,” and بدکنش is an adjective/noun meaning someone who behaves ill or ill‑conduct (here used as ‘ill‑behaviour’).
segments.s661-g59: چنین = “thus/so,” داد (here) = the verb “gave” used with پاسخ “answer” to form “gave an answer” (narrative past). که introduces the content clause of the reply (“that/that which is …”). آز and نیاز are the two nouns named in the reply: “greed” and “need/desire.”
segments.s661-g60: The predicate is elliptical (هستند/هست omitted): دو = “two,” دیوند = a demon/fiend (poetic form), and the two adjectives بد‑گوهر and دیر‑ساز describe those fiends. بد‌گوهر literally “bad‑germ/evil‑essenced” = “evil in nature,” and دیر‌ساز = “one who makes things late / causes delay.” The adjectives are linked by و and modify دیوند.
segments.s661-g61: هرآنکس که introduces a conditional/relative: “whoever (that) …”. بیشی کند uses بیشی (“excess, too much”) with the verb کند (present/habitual “does/acts”) to mean “indulges in excess / acts with excess”; آرزوی (object) = “a wish/desire.” Word order places the object (آرزوی) after the verb for poetic rhythm.
segments.s661-g62: بدو = “to it/unto that” (referring back to the demon), دیو = “demon/fiend.” او here functions as a possessive/pronominal modifier (“his/its”) tied to دیو (“his demon” or “that demon” in context). باز گردد is a compound verb meaning “turn back / return” (باز = again; گردد = become/turn), and به خوی = “to his disposition/nature,” so the clause says the wish returns to the demon and the person reverts to his own nature.
segments.s661-g63: وگر = “but if / and if” introducing a conditional. سفلگی = “baseness/lowliness.” برگزید is the past/simple form “chose/selected” (used here as the conditional clause’s verb). او = “he.” ز رنج = “from/by hardship” (ز = poetic “from/by,” رنج = “hardship/toil”), indicating that hardship accompanies or is the means by which the choice plays out.
segments.s661-g64: گزیند is a present/future‑oriented form of گزین‑دن “choose” (here: “he will choose / he chooses”). برین is the fused form of بر + این = “on/over this” or “this (very)”; خاک = “earth/soil/land”; آگنده = “filled/laden”; گنج = “treasure.” Together: “he will choose this land filled with treasure,” with the sense that through hardship he attains that treasure‑laden earth.
segments.s661-g393: چنان هم که is a comparative introducer meaning 'just so/just as' (چنان = 'so', هم adds emphasis 'also/indeed', که introduces the clause). از here marks a genitive/partitive relation 'of' or 'from' and attaches to داد نوشین‌روان, describing a person 'of justice, sweet‑souled' (داد used as a moral quality 'just'/'justice' and نوشین‌روان is a compound adjective 'sweet‑souled'). Word order places the descriptive nouns/adjectives after از to characterize the referenced person.
segments.s661-g394: کجا is literal 'where' (pointing to the place of burial), خاک شد is a light verb construction 'became earth/dust' (شد = past 'became'), and ماندش is the past 'remained' with the enclitic -ش = 'to him/of him' so that نام (subject) + ماندش + جوان means 'his name remained young/fresh.' Word order here is subject (نام) + verb with clitic (ماندش) + predicative adjective (جوان).
segments.s661-g241: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed narrative phrase 'he gave such an answer' (چنین = 'thus', داد = past 'gave', پاسخ = 'answer'); که introduces the reported content. هم‌سنگ کوه is a predicate phrase meaning 'equal to a mountain' — هم‌سنگ is a compound adjective 'equal/on a par with' and کوه ('mountain') follows it without the ezāfe (omitted in poetic diction).
segments.s661-g242: جز introduces an exception: 'except'. اندوه مشمر is a noun plus participial/adjectival word: اندوه 'sorrow' modified by مشمر (poetic/archaic participle meaning 'girded/armed/wrapped'), with the adjective after the noun as is common in Persian poetry. که begins a relative clause 'which/that', گرد (گردد) is the classical third-person present/subjunctive 'becomes' or 'turns', and ستوه is the predicate adjective 'weary/wearied'.
segments.s661-g243: چه بیمست is a rhetorical formula 'what (is there) to fear?' (چه = 'what', بیمست = 'there is fear' / 'is fear' in an idiomatic sense). اگر introduces the condition 'if'. بیم اندوه نیست can be read as 'there is no fear (of) sorrow' or 'if fear is not sorrow' — بیم 'fear' followed by اندوه 'sorrow', with نیست negating the clause.
segments.s661-g244: به گیتی = 'in the world' (به used poetically with گیتی 'world'). جز introduces the exclusive expression 'except'. اندوه نستوه is 'sorrow' qualified by the adjective نستوه ('distressed/unquiet'); the final نیست negates the existential: idiomatically, 'there is nothing in the world except unquiet sorrow.'
segments.s661-g245: بپرسید is a past narrative verb 'they asked' (classical past). کز ما که functions as 'which of us who/that' (کز = 'which/of' here, ما = 'we'). با گنج‌تر is a descriptive phrase 'with more treasure' — با 'with' plus گنج 'treasure' with the comparative suffix -تر meaning 'richer'.
segments.s661-g246: چنین گفت = 'said thus' (چنین 'thus', گفت 'said' — reported speech). کان کس که introduces the quoted content with an archaic demonstrative/relative (کان here introduces 'the one who / that person who'); که begins a relative clause. بی‌رنج‌تر is a comparative adjective 'less afflicted / freer of suffering' (بی- 'without' + رنج 'suffering' + comparative -تر). Note: کان is an archaic element; word order reflects poetic compression.
segments.s661-g247: بپرسید که = 'they asked that/asked' (introducing an indirect question). او کدامست is 'which one is he?' (او 'he', کدامست = archaic fused form of 'کدام است' 'which is it'), and زشت is the predicate 'ugly / vile'. The structure is a direct/indirect question in poetic diction.
segments.s661-g248: که here is the relative 'who'. از ارج دورست = 'is distant from honor' (از = 'from', ارج = 'honor', دورست = 'is distant' — note the adjective + copula form). و دور از بهشت repeats the idea 'and far from paradise' (دور 'far', از = 'from', بهشت = 'paradise'), giving a parallel pair. The repetition and slightly different forms (دورست vs. دور) are poetic stylistic choices.
segments.s661-g137: بپرسید is an imperative / hortative form meaning “ask” or “inquire” addressed to a listener (polite/plural). It is followed by a temporal clause introduced by تا (“when/until”), with تو شدی شهریار as the subordinate clause: تو (subject) شدی (past, “you became”) شهریار (predicate noun “king”). The clause together means “ask (about it) when you became king.”
segments.s661-g138: This is a verbless interrogative clause in which the noun سپاس (“praise/thanks”) is qualified by the adjective/marker فزون (“greater/more”), followed by the inverted copula چیست (“what is”) and the prepositional phrase از کردگار (“from/of the Creator”). The surface order places the quantified noun first (سپاست فزون) and then asks چیست از کردگار — “what greater praise is there of the Creator?”
segments.s661-g139: کزان is a prepositional/relational compound meaning “from that/whence.” مر is the archaic/dialectal form for ‘to me’ (modern به من), and تو را is the object phrase ‘of you’ (تو marked by را). The predicate دانش افزون شده‌ست is a perfect/resultative construction: دانش (knowledge) + افزون (“increased/more”) + شده‌ست (“has become/has grown”), i.e. “my knowledge of you has increased.”
segments.s661-g140: دل بدسگالان is a genitive/compound: دل (“heart”) + بدسگالان (plural “the ill‑disposed / ill‑fated ones”), and پر از خون شده‌ست is a resultative clause: پر از (“full of”) + خون (“blood”) + شده‌ست (“has become/has been”), meaning “the hearts of the ill‑disposed have become full of blood.” Word order places the possessed noun (دل) before its possessor phrase (بدسگالان) and the predicate follows.
segments.s661-g141: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrasing meaning “gave such an answer” (چنین = thus/such, داد = gave, پاسخ = answer). The conjunction/marker که introduces the content or explanatory phrase that follows; از کردگار is a prepositional phrase “from/of the Creator.” Grammatically, the clause appoints the following phrase as the content or reason of the reply.
segments.s661-g142: سپاس آنک is “thanks that/for,” where آنک is a classical connective meaning “that” or “for the reason that.” گشتیم is first‑person plural past “we became” or “we have turned/come to be,” and به روزگار here means “by fate / in the course of time” (به + روزگار). The whole clause is a thankful statement: “thanks that we have come to be by fate.”
segments.s661-g143: کسی پیش من means “anyone before me / in my presence,” with پیش indicating precedence/presence. بر فزونی نجست uses بر + فزونی (= “toward/exceeding/over‑superiority”) and نجست (classical past form of جستن used as “leapt/sprang”); together it conveys “no one leapt/strived to surpass me.” The negative sense is implied by the poetic context (“کسی ... نجست” = nobody did).
segments.s661-g144: وز here is the cliticized classical conjunctional form (equivalent to و از = “and from/through”), introducing the means or cause: آواز من (“my voice”). دست بد را is the direct object (دست “hand” + بد “bad/evil” + را object marker). بشست is past “he washed.” The clause thus literally reads “and by my voice he washed the bad hand,” with object preceding the verb, a normal classical Persian order.
segments.s661-g169: ندارد is a present negative verbal phrase 'does not have / does not spare'. The object تن خویش ('his own body') follows the verb; خوی + ش is the older split of the possessive form خویش ('his own'), with ش the possessive suffix. با رنج و درد is an instrumental/accompaniment phrase 'with toil and pain' modifying how the body is (i.e. what it endures).
segments.s661-g170: جهان را نگهبان is the object (جهان) marked by را plus the role 'guardian/protector' (نگهبان). هرآنکس که کرد is a relative clause 'whoever made/did so' with کرد in the simple past; together: 'whoever made the world (his) guardian'. The clause as a whole supplies the agent referred to by the main verb ندارد.
segments.s661-g171: بپرسید is a past-tense reportive/third-person form 'asked' (classical usage). شادان دل is a compound adjective-noun 'joyful-hearted' placed before the noun شهریار 'king', so 'the king whose heart is joyful' or 'the joyful-hearted king.'
segments.s661-g172: پر اندیشه is an adjectival phrase 'full of thought' (پر = 'full (of)', followed by the noun اندیشه 'thought'). بینم is first-person present 'I see' — the narrator interjects. بدین روزگار is a frozen prepositional phrase 'in these times' (بدین = به + دین here functioning as the demonstrative/compound 'this/these' with the preposition ب-), modifying the whole observation.
segments.s661-g173: چنین داد پاسخ is an idiomatic, somewhat archaic sequence 'thus gave an answer' (داد پاسـخ = 'gave reply'); که introduces the subordinate clause ('that ...'). بیم گزند is the noun phrase 'fear of harm' serving as the theme of the reported clause.
segments.s661-g174: ندارد is the negated present verb 'does not have'. به دل is a locative/use phrase 'in the heart', where به+دل = 'in the heart of'. مردم هوشمند is a noun + postpositive adjective 'the intelligent people' (هوشمند postmodifies مردم), so the whole clause reads 'does not have (it) in the heart of the wise/ intelligent people.'
segments.s661-g175: بدو گفت is an inverted classical order 'said to him' (بدو = 'to him', placed before the verb). شاهان پیشین is the subject 'the former/ancient kings.' ز بزم ('from the feast' / 'regarding the banquet') begins the matter about which they speak; ز is the poetic preposition 'from/because of'.
segments.s661-g176: نبردند is a past negative verb 'they did not ...' (classical past plural). جان را is the object 'their life(s)' (را marks the definite object). به‌اندازه رزم is a comparative/measure phrase 'to the extent of battle' or 'as much as in war' — together: 'they did not put their lives at stake at feasts to the extent they did in battle.'
segments.s661-g249: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed narrative phrase: داد (past 'gave') takes پاسخ ('an answer') and is placed before the reported clause. The following که introduces that clause; زن‌را (woman + object marker -را) is the head of the relative clause and the second که begins the relative 'who...' that continues into the next segment (شرم …).
segments.s661-g250: نباشد is the negative present/subjunctive 3rd-person of 'to be' meaning 'is not / has not' and attaches back to شرم (so: 'who has no shame/modesty'). به گیتی 'in the world' is a prepositional phrase; نه + noun (نه آواز نرم) negates the subsequent nominal phrase ('no/generally not a gentle voice'), with آواز (voice) modified postpositively by نرم ('gentle').
segments.s661-g251: ز here functions as the poetic comparative 'than' (classical variant of از), so 'ز مردان بتر' = 'worse than men.' آنک is a relative particle ('that/who') introducing the clause 'نادان بود' ('was ignorant'); بود is the past copula 'was.' Word order is inverted from modern colloquial Persian for poetic effect.
segments.s661-g252: همه زندگانی means 'the whole/entire life' (همه as a distributive/totalizing modifier before the noun). به زندان بود uses به + place noun to express being 'in / as if in' prison, with بود as the past copula 'was.' The clause states his life was, in effect, a prison.
segments.s661-g253: بگرود is an archaic/perfect form of گرویدن ('to turn to, resort to, embrace'), here past: 'he turned to.' به یزدان is the prepositional object 'to God.' The coordinating و links this clause to the following noun phrase تن پرگناه ('sinful body'), which functions as the subject/topic for the next verb phrase.
segments.s661-g254: This clause contains the result: بدی ('evil') is what was done, بر دل خویش ('upon his own heart') marks the target (بر + noun phrase). خویش is split into خوی + ش (kinship/root + 3rd-person possessive) giving 'his own.' کرده is the perfect/past participial form 'having done' / 'had done,' and سیاه is a consequence adjective 'black/dark' describing the heart's state.
segments.s661-g255: بپرسید here is the past narrative verb 'he asked' (verb precedes the object in this poetic ordering): 'he asked the people.' کدامست راست is the content question/nominal clause ('which is right'): کدامست = 'which is it' (کدام + archaic copula -ست), and راست is the predicate 'right/true.'
segments.s661-g256: که introduces an explanatory/reason clause: 'for' or 'because.' جان و خرد ('life and wisdom') are the nominative items that 'testify' — بر دل او 'upon his heart' gives the locus, and گواست is an archaic fused form meaning 'is witness / testifies' (گوا + است). The clause states that life and wisdom serve as witnesses written on his heart.
segments.s661-g65: چو functions as a temporal conjunction 'when'. The phrase بیچاره دیوی is the subject (an adjectival + noun: 'the wretched demon'), and بود is the past copula 'was'. دیرساز is a predicate noun meaning 'one who causes delay' placed after the copula. The enclitic -ی on دیو is a genitive/attributive/nominalizing element common in older Persian, but the main copula is بود.
segments.s661-g66: که introduces a subordinate/relative clause ('that' / 'where'). هر دو = 'both' (subject). به یک خو is a prepositional phrase 'toward the same خو'; here خو means 'habit, disposition' rather than 'sleep'. گرایند is a third-person plural present (they incline/turn) used in narrative to state tendency, and باز means 'again' (i.e. they revert). Word order is literal: 'that both to one-habit incline again.'
segments.s661-g67: بپرسید and گفتا are two past-tense verbs in sequence ('he asked and said'); the subject is omitted as usual. که introduces the reported question. چندست is the interrogative 'how many / how much' (here 'how many / how large [is it]'), and چی + ست ('what' + copula) forms 'what is it?'.
segments.s661-g68: که continues the subordinate/question clause. بهری (بهر + ی) acts dative/benefactive 'for its/his sake' (i.e. 'for that'), برو is the verb 'to go' (here infinitival/ purposive), هم = 'also', and بباید + گریست expresses obligation: بباید 'one must/ought' and گریست (classical infinitive/old form of 'to weep') completes the predicate 'one must weep'. The order is somewhat archaic but typical of classical Persian: 'that for its sake one must also go and weep.'
segments.s661-g69: دگر introduces a contrast 'on the other hand / another (thing)'. بهر ازو = 'from him / for him' (بهر as a preposition + ازو 'from him'). گنج و تاجست و نام lists nouns 'treasure and (it is) a crown and name/fame' with the copula -ست attached to تاج; in classical Persian the copula often attaches to the last element of a coordinate phrase and governs the whole predicate.
segments.s661-g70: ازان 'of those / from those' refers back to the previously named items (treasure, crown, name). مستمندیم is first-person plural present 'we are needy / lacking' (copula included in the verbal form). The phrase و زین شادکام uses زین ('these trappings' / 'these things') + شادکام 'fortunate/happy' as a predicate adjective; the copula is omitted (typical in Persian), so it reads 'and by these trappings (we are) joyful.'
segments.s661-g71: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase 'thus gave an answer' (چنین 'thus', داد 'gave', پاسخ 'answer'). که introduces the content clause of the answer. دانا سخن is a noun phrase 'the wise (one)' + 'speech/word' preparing the verbal action that follows.
segments.s661-g72: ببخشید here is past tense 'bestowed/gave' (not merely 'forgave') and applies to سخن: 'bestowed words' or 'uttered words'. و links the next action. اندیشه افگند is 'he cast/laid his thought' (اندیشه = thought; افگند = past of افکندن 'to cast/lay'); بن (بن) is 'foundation/base' and serves adverbially: 'cast his thought upon the foundation' — i.e. he set his mind firmly. Word order places the verb after its object, as normal in classical Persian.
segments.s661-g89: بپرسید is a past narrative 3rd-person form (‘he asked’) used to introduce reported speech; چندی here functions as an interrogative/adverb meaning ‘how long’; که introduces the subordinate clause and is the conjunction 'that/when'; آموختی is 2nd-person singular past of آموختن, ‘you learned’ (here part of the reported question).
segments.s661-g90: روان را is the direct object (‘the soul’ + object marker را). به دانش is a prepositional phrase ‘to/for knowledge’. بیفروختی is a past 2nd-person form meaning ‘(did) not sell’ here (classical/poetic negation with بی-), giving the sense ‘did you not sell?’ in the reported question.
segments.s661-g91: چنین گفت is a formulaic phrase ‘thus he said’ (چنین = ‘thus’). کز is the fused form of که + از and here introduces the source ('from whom/which'); هرک is an indefinite relative ‘whomever/whichever’; آموختم is 1st-person singular past ‘I learned’ and heads the subordinate clause.
segments.s661-g92: همه modifies فام as a quantifier ‘all/every (hue)’. فام here is a poetic noun ‘hue/trace’. جان و خرد are coordinated objects ‘of life (soul) and of wisdom’. توختم is an archaic 1st-person past verb (here ‘I took/imbued/appropriated’); its exact lexical sense is archaic/obscure but it is the main verb completing the clause.
segments.s661-g93: همی‌پرسم is a present/imperfect habitual verb ‘I keep asking’ or ‘I frequently ask’. از + ناسزایان سخن marks the topic/object ‘about the speech of the revilers/insulters’ (از here = ‘concerning’), with سخن = ‘speech/words’.
segments.s661-g94: چه گویی is a direct question ‘what do you say?’ where چه = ‘what’ and گویی is the 2nd-person present/potential verbal form ‘you say’ (here used interrogatively). که introduces the subordinate interrogative clause; دانش کی آید به بن is a nested question ‘when will knowledge come to the root/foundation?’ (کی = ‘when’, آید = ‘come’, به بن = ‘to the root’).
segments.s661-g95: به دانش نگر is an imperative instruction ‘look to/consider knowledge’ (به = ‘to/at’, نگر = imperative ‘look!’). دور باش از گناه is a second imperative clause ‘be far from sin’ (دور باش = ‘be distant’, از گناه = ‘from sin’); the two imperatives are coordinated advice.
segments.s661-g96: که introduces the reason ‘for’ or ‘because’. دانش is the subject ‘knowledge/learning’. گرامی‌تر is the comparative adjective ‘more honored/dearer’. از introduces the standard of the comparison ‘than’; تاج و گاه are the compared items ‘crown and time/occasion’ (گاه here = ‘time/occasion’).
segments.s661-g25: بگفتش is a past verb with the enclitic -ش (“to him/her”), literally “he said to him/her.” خرد را is the topical/definite object marked by را (here ‘Wisdom’ as addressee/topic). که introduces the indirect question clauseچی ست = “what is” (چی = what, ست = copula); the whole chunk means “He said to Wisdom what the foundation is.”
segments.s661-g26: به‌شاخ و به‌برگ are two locative/partitive prepositional phrases (“on the branch and on the leaf”) joined by و. خرد شاد کیست is an interrogative clause where کیست (کی + است) is the copula + interrogative ‘who is’; the phrase asks “who is glad/rejoicing at (the) branch and leaf(s) of wisdom.”
segments.s661-g27: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase “thus gave answer” (داد = gave, پاسخ = answer). که introduces the reported content. داناست is an archaic fused copula/adjective form meaning “is wise” (دانا + است), and شاد is the predicate noun/adjective “happy”; word order yields “the happy one is the wise.”
segments.s661-g28: دگر آنک begins a contrasting clause: دگر “another” + آنک (archaic relative “that/who”), introducing “another (one) who …”. شرمش is شرم + possessive -ش “his shame/modesty.” بود is past copula “was” (here functioning as simple copula), and با نژاد “with/by lineage” qualifies the shame — i.e. ‘one whose modesty is by birth/lineage.’
segments.s661-g29: بپرسید is a past-tense verb “he asked.” دانش کرا places the noun دانش “knowledge” before the archaic object/indirect question pronoun کرا (“to whom/whom”), and سودمند is the predicative adjective “useful” (copula omitted). Together: “He asked, to whom is knowledge useful?”
segments.s661-g30: کدامست is the interrogative ‘which/which is it’ (کدام + است). بی‌دانش و بی‌گزند are two conjoined adjectival phrases (“ignorant and unharmed/harmless”) that present the candidate answer; the verb is contained in کدامست, so the clause reads “Is it the ignorant and the unharmed?”
segments.s661-g31: چنین داد پاسخ again = “he answered thus.” که introduces the reported clause. هر کو is an archaic conditional/relative “whoever” (هر + کو/که), with خرد “wisdom” placed before its verb (topicalized) — i.e. “whoever (هر کو) cultivates wisdom…”
segments.s661-g32: بپرورد is a verb meaning “to nurture/cultivate” (here part of the conditional clause). جان را is the definite object “the soul” marked by را, and همی‌پرورد is the present/formal continuous poetic form “(he) also nurtures.” The pair says: “if one cultivates wisdom, he also cultivates the soul.”
segments.s661-g257: چنین گفت کانکو = 'thus said Kanku.' چنين (چنین) is an adverb 'thus/so' placed before گفت (said). کانکو is the speaker's name placed after گفت in inverted/poetic word order. به سود و زیان is a prepositional phrase 'with regard to profit and loss' (به + two nouns joined by و) that qualifies what one should not base speech or judgment on.
segments.s661-g258: نگوید نبندد بدی را میان is an admonition in reported speech. نگوید here functions as a negative imperative / jussive 'do not say' or 'let it not be said.' نبندد is a negative jussive/optative meaning 'do not close/shut' (نبند + ـد), and بدی را میان (بدی + definite object marker را + میان 'between') means 'evil' as the object placed 'between' — i.e. 'do not let evil be shut/hidden between [people].' The clause reads as two linked prohibitions.
segments.s661-g259: بپرسید کزو خو چه نیکوترست = 'Ask, from whom which disposition is better.' بپرسید is an imperative 'ask' (poetic form placed before its complement). کزو = 'from whom / of whom' introduces the source/agent asked about. خو (خوی) here means 'disposition/character.' چه is an interrogative 'which/what' or 'how,' and نیکوترست is the comparative predicate 'is better' (نیکوتر + است contracted).
segments.s661-g260: که آن بر سر مردمان افسر‌ست = 'for that is a diadem upon the heads of people.' که introduces an explanatory clause ('for' / 'that'). آن is the demonstrative 'that (quality/thing).' بر سر is the prepositional phrase 'on/upon the head(s) of' and سر is literal 'head' used figuratively of status. مردمان = 'people,' and افسر‌ست is a nominal predicate 'is a diadem/helmet' (افسر = diadem/helmet; -ست = 'is').
segments.s661-g261: چنین داد پاسخ که چون بردبار = 'He gave this answer: that when (a man is) patient...' چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrasing 'thus gave answer' (چنین 'thus' + داد 'gave' + پاسخ 'answer'). که introduces the subordinate clause. چون here functions temporally/causally 'when' (or 'since') and is followed by the adjective بردبار 'patient; forbearing.'
segments.s661-g262: بود مرد نایدش افسون به کار = 'a man was (such) that enchantment does not affect him' (literally: 'was — man — it does not come to him, enchantment, to work'). بود is the past copula 'was.' مرد is the subject 'man.' نایدش is an impersonal/Expletive negative construction meaning 'does not come to him / does not affect him' (ناید + ـش). افسون is 'enchantment/charm' as the thing that would act, and به کار is a prepositional phrase 'to/into effect' = 'to work/affect.'
segments.s661-g263: نه آن کز پی سودمندی کند = 'Not that one who follows/in pursuit of advantage.' نه آن is a negating demonstrative 'not that one.' کز is the relative 'who/that (from/that which).' پی + noun is a classical Persian prepositional construction meaning 'in pursuit of / after' (here پی سودمندی = 'in pursuit of advantage/usefulness'). کند is the present habitual/gnomic verb 'does / acts (thus).'
segments.s661-g264: وگر نیز رای بلندی کند = 'nor even one who (still) devises lofty counsel.' وگر is concessive 'and if / even if' used here to continue the negation ('nor even...'). نیز = 'also / even.' رای is 'opinion / plan / counsel' (here 'counsel, scheme'), بلندی is an abstract noun 'loftiness / high-mindedness' modifying رای ('high-minded counsel'), and کند is again the verb 'does / devises.' The whole phrase contrasts types of people who are excluded by the prior negation.
segments.s661-g177: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed poetic order meaning “gave such an answer” (literally “thus gave answer”); داد is past tense 3rd‑singular 'gave'. The conjunction که introduces the reported clause that follows. ایشان is the third‑person plural subject 'they'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from'/'because of' and attaches to جام 'cup' (so: 'because of the cup').
segments.s661-g178: نکردند is the past negative plural ('they did not do'); here it combines with یاد کردن (to remember) so the sense is 'they did not remember'. هرگز is the adverb 'never' reinforcing the negation. به دل is an adverbial phrase 'into the heart' (literally 'to the heart'). یاد نام is a noun phrase 'remembrance of the name' (یاد = memory, named is specified by نام), so the clause means 'they never put the name into their heart'.
segments.s661-g179: مرا نام is an archaic/poetic order for 'my name' (مرا as a preposed possessive/object-like form + نام). بر جام چیره شده‌ست: چیره شدن بر + object = 'to prevail over/overcome' (چیره here ≈ 'dominant/overcame'); شده‌ست is the perfect/result state auxiliary 'has become/has been', so the whole predicate says the name 'has become dominant over the cup' (i.e. reputation outlasts wine).
segments.s661-g180: روانم is 'my soul/spirit' (روان + possessive -م). زمان‌را (time + definite marker را) is the object. پذیره شده‌ست is a passive/result phrase meaning 'has been receptive/has accepted' (پذیره = reception/accepted, شده‌ست = 'has become/been'), so syntactically 'my soul has accepted/welcomed time' (i.e. the spirit has taken on/received the era/time).
segments.s661-g181: بپرسید here functions as an exhortation/imperative 'ask' (classical Persian uses the prefix بـ plus the stem for jussive/imperative). هرکس 'whoever/anyone' heads the relative/subordinate clause; که introduces that clause ('who/that'). شاهان is 'kings'; بدند is an archaic past plural form of بودن ('they were' / in context a general past habitual 'they would be'), so the clause names those persons: 'who were kings' or 'who were among the kings'.
segments.s661-g182: تن خویشتن را is the direct object phrase 'their own body' (تن = body, خویشتن emphatic 'self/own', را marks definiteness/object). نگهبان بدند is a verbal phrase where نگهبان functions as predicate 'a guardian' and بدند is the past plural; together the sense is 'they made/set (it) as their guardian' or loosely 'they made their body their guardian.' In older Persian بدند can appear where modern Persian would use 'گذاشتند' or 'ساختند' to mean 'made/appointed'.
segments.s661-g183: به is the preposition 'to/with/for' introducing means or direction. The coordinated nouns دارو و درمان و کار پزشک list remedies and the physician's activity: دارو 'medicine', درمان 'treatment/cure', and کار پزشک 'the physician’s work/skill' (noun + modifier). The conjunction و links the items in a standard coordinate series.
segments.s661-g184: بدان is the composite of the prefix بـ + دان 'know/consider' and here functions as an imperative/command 'know this' or 'attend to this / apply (these measures)'. تا introduces purpose 'so that / in order that'. نپالود is the negative form of پالود (from پالودن 'to purify/cleanse') and here means 'not be purified' ≈ 'not be spoiled/defiled' (the negation + verb give the undesirable outcome). باید is the modal 'must/ought', and سرشک 'tear/teardrop' is the thing that must not be spoiled — overall: 'Attend to (medicine, treatment, physician's work) so that the teardrop must not be ruined.'
segments.s661-g217: This clause is an imperative/declarative instruction: 'شناس' (an older poetic form of 'شناختن') functions as 'know/consider'. 'ز' here precedes the comparative adjective and works like 'than'/'of' in classical usage; 'گیتی' = 'the world'. 'زبون‌تر' is the comparative adjective 'more base/abject' (زبون + -تر). 'مر' is the dative/enclitic 'to me' and 'آن را' is the definite object 'that one' (را as the object marker). Word order is topical/object then predicate: 'From-the-world more-abject to-me that-OBJ know.'
segments.s661-g218: 'که' introduces the relative clause 'who...'. 'نیکی' here means 'kindness/good deed'. 'سگالید' is a poetic verb meaning to assail/ill-treat (here metaphorically 'to repay/turn against'); it describes how kindness is treated. 'با' = 'with', and 'ناسپاس' = 'ungrateful/thankless' describes the manner of the treatment. The whole relative clause modifies 'آن' (that person).
segments.s661-g219: 'بپرسید' is a past-tense (narrative) verb 'he asked'. 'کان کس' is an archaic indefinite phrase meaning 'anyone' or 'was there a person'; here it introduces the question. 'که' begins a relative clause 'who...'; 'بد کرد' is the past 'did evil/acted badly'. The following 'و مرد' is the past of 'مردن' ('died') in context (verb), i.e. 'and died' (the provided noun senses for 'مرد' do not include this verbal use).
segments.s661-g220: 'ز' here means 'from'/'out of' (classical poetic use). 'دیوان جهان' = 'the rolls/registers of the world' (دیوان in the sense of a register or record). 'نام' = 'name' but in context more 'reputation/fame'. 'او' = 'his', and 'را' is the object marker. 'سترد' is used in the sense 'to blot out / conceal / remove' (poetic usage), so the phrase says his name is blotted from the world's record.
segments.s661-g221: 'هران کس' = 'each/every person' (هران = every/each). 'که' introduces the relative clause 'who'. 'نیکی کند' is the present/habitual 'does good' (کند as the present-tense verb). 'بگذرد' is a present/future-oriented verb 'passes' (here 'his time passes' / 'his time comes to an end'); the verb is syntactically tied to the following clause that names the agent 'زمانه' doing the reckoning.
segments.s661-g222: 'زمانه' (time/age/fate) is the subject of the verb that follows. 'نفس' here means 'breath' / 'life-breath' (i.e., a person's life). 'را' is the object marker. 'همی‌بشمرد' (compound with the prefixed habitual particle همی-) is present/habitual 'counts' or 'reckons'. Together: 'When his time passes, Time will count (take account of) his breath (life)'.
segments.s661-g223: 'چه' here functions rhetorically as 'why'/'what (then)'; 'باید' = 'ought / it is necessary'; 'همی' is a poetic continuous/emphatic particle (here giving immediacy: 'should one still'); 'نیکویی را' is the object 'goodness/virtue' (را marks it definite); and 'ستود' is the infinitive/gerund 'to praise' (the verb governed by باید). The clause poses the question why virtue should be praised given what follows.
segments.s661-g224: 'چو' = 'when'. 'مرگ آمد' = 'Death came' (simple past narrative). The second half is elliptical/poetic: 'نیک و بد را درود' — 'نیک و بد' = 'the good and the bad' and 'درود' = 'a greeting' (here 'death greets both good and bad'); the verb is implied or folded into the clause, a common poetic compression.
segments.s661-g225: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed poetic sequence meaning “he answered thus.” چنین is an adverb “thus/so” placed before the verb; داد (here the verb “gave”) takes پاسخ (“answer/reply”) as its object. که introduces the reported content (a complementizer “that”), and کردار نیک is the subject noun phrase of that content (کردار = “deed/action,” نیک = “good/virtuous”).
segments.s661-g226: بیابد is third-person singular present/subjunctive/formal present of یافتن ‘to find’ and here translates “finds” or “will find.” به هر جای is a locative prepositional phrase (“to/into/in every place”), بازار نیک means “the good market/marketplace” (بازار = place of trade/public space, نیک = good). Together the verb and prepositional phrase give the clause “finds (a place) in every good market.”
