segments.s661-g227: نمرد is the negative past/present-formal expression meaning “does not die / did not die” (here idiomatically “does not perish”). آنکه introduces a relative clause “he who….” او is the emphatic personal pronoun “he.” نیک کردار is an attributive phrase “of good deeds,” and مرد here is a noun “man/person,” so the relative clause reads “he who was a man of good deeds.” The whole clause is the complement of نمرد: “He does not die who is a man of good deeds.”
segments.s661-g228: بیاسود is a predicate describing the same person, here meaning “found rest/was at peace” (poetic usage of آسودن). و links the next clause, and جان را به یزدان سپرد is a transitive clause: جان (the soul/life) marked by the object marker را is the object of سپرد (past tense “entrusted/handed over”), with به یزدان = “to God.”
segments.s661-g229: وزان is the compound of و + زان meaning “and from that/of that,” introducing a new thought. کس که ماند همی is a relative clause “the person whose … remains” (هرکس = “any person/whoever” in similar lines; here کس = “person”), with ماند “remains” and همی emphasizing continuity (“still/continually”). نام بد is the noun phrase “bad name/reputation” (نام used here as ‘reputation’). The whole segment means “and of that person whose bad name endures….”
segments.s661-g230: از آغاز بد بود is a simple predication “(he) was bad from the beginning,” with از آغاز = “from the start” and بود the past copula “was.” و links the next parallel phrase فرجام بد “bad end/outcome,” so the second half reads “and (his) end was bad,” the verbless nominal phrase understood in parallel with the first clause.
segments.s661-g231: نیاسود is negative of آسودن (“is not at rest / is uneasy”). هرکس introduces “whoever/no one” as the indefinite subject. کزو is a contracted relative/pronominal form meaning “from him/from which,” and باز ماند is a verbal phrase “remains behind / is left.” Put together: “No one is at peace who is left behind by him” (i.e. whoever is left behind is not at rest).
segments.s661-g232: وزو (و + زو) functions here as “and from him.” در زمانه بد is a locative phrase “in a bad/evil age,” and آواز ماند is a verbless clause meaning “a report/voice/reputation remains” (آواز used poetically as ‘reputation’). The segment says that from him, in bad times, an ill reputation endures.
segments.s661-g41: "چنین گفت" is a fixed formula meaning "he thus said/so said" (literally "thus said"). "زان پس" is a compressed classical phrase = "from that, after that" (i.e. "then/afterwards"); "که" introduces the content clause that follows. "زیبای تخت" is an adjectival phrase in classical word order (adjective + noun) meaning "the beautiful/fairest throne." The whole segment sets up a reported question that continues in the next segment.
segments.s661-g42: This is an interrogative continuation. "کدامست" = "which is it?" introduces the question. "وز" here is the poetic contraction of "و از" ("and from/and whose") — an archaic link between the two interrogatives. "کیست" = "who/whose is it" ("کی" = who, plus copula "ست"). "ناشاد بخت" is a two‑word phrase with the adjective before the noun (classical order) meaning "ill‑fated / unhappy fortune." Together the line asks two linked questions: which throne is (the beautiful one), and whose is the unhappy fate?
segments.s661-g43: "چنین داد پاسخ" literally "thus gave answer" = "he answered thus." The conjunction "که" introduces the content of the answer. "یاری نخست" is the direct object phrase "the first help/assistance," with "نخست" marking priority ('first').
segments.s661-g44: "بباید ... جست" expresses necessity: "one must seek." In classical Persian the modal "بباید" (it is necessary / one must) is followed by a verbal stem (here "جست" used as the action "to seek"). "ز شاه جهاندار" means "from the king, the world‑holder/sovereign" with the poetic preposition "ز" = "از." Word order places source before the verb ("from the king ... to seek").
segments.s661-g45: "دگر" introduces another item in a list ("another"/"next"). The three nouns are coordinated by "و": "بخشش" (generosity/bounty), "دانش" (knowledge), and "رسم گاه" (literally "custom/time" — here "observance of rite / proper conduct at the proper time"). Classical Persian often strings virtues this way with simple coordination.
segments.s661-g46: This clause has an implied copula (بود) that is customary in poetic/classical Persian and thus is omitted: "دلش پر ز بخشایش دادخواه" = "his heart, full of generosity, was a seeker of justice." "دلش" = "his heart" (دِل + enclitic ش), "پر ز" = "full of" with source introduced by "ز" (from/of), and "دادخواه" is a nominal predicate meaning "one who seeks justice / a petitioner for justice."
segments.s661-g47: "ششم نیز" enumerates the sixth item ("sixth too"). "کانرا" is a demonstrative object ("that [thing]"), placed before the verb. "دهد" is the third‑person present/subjunctive form "he gives/grants." "مهتری" here denotes "preeminence / precedence" (the thing being granted).
segments.s661-g48: "که" introduces a relative clause describing who receives that preeminence. "باشد" is the classical subjunctive/present of "بودن" used here as the copula ("who is / who may be"). "سزوار بر بهتری" = "worthy of superiority/preeminence," where "سزوار" = "worthy" and the preposition "بر" takes "بهتر/بهتری" ("the better/one of higher rank"); this whole clause qualifies the recipient. The copula is explicit here in the subjunctive mood, a frequent poetic usage.
segments.s661-g81: چهارم is the enumerative noun 'the fourth' and is followed by a relative clause introduced by که ('who/whom'). دانا is the agent 'the wise (one)', دلارای is the name/epithet given, and خواند is a past, transitive verb here meaning 'called' or 'named'. The clause literally reads 'the fourth whom the wise called Dilarây.'
segments.s661-g82: سراینده را is the definite object ('the poet' marked by را). مرد is the predicate noun 'man' (here with force 'man/valiant man') and بارای is a prepositional/attributive particle attached to مرد (an epithet-like construction). خواند repeats the past transitive 'called' — so the whole segment says '(he) called the poet a man Barây' (i.e., gave him that epithet).
segments.s661-g83: که introduces another relative clause 'who'. پیوسته is an adverb 'constantly/continually' placed before the verb, گوید is the verb 'says/utters' (a habitual/presentive form here), and سراسر سخن is the object phrase 'the whole speech/tale' — note the poet permits slight inversion for meter but function is adverb + verb + object.
segments.s661-g84: اگر introduces a conditional 'if/even if'. نو qualifies the (understood) 'story' as 'new'. بود is the past form of 'to be' ('was' / conditional sense), and داستان‌گر کهن is the predicate nominal 'an old (seasoned) storyteller' referring back to the subject of the relative clause. Together: 'even if the tale is new, he is an old storyteller.'
segments.s661-g85: به پنجم is a topical/enumerative tag 'as for the fifth'. که باشد begins a relative clause 'who is' (باشد is the third-person present/subjunctive form of 'to be' used here as 'is'), سخنگوی is the predicate noun 'a speaker', and گرم qualifies it as 'warm/ardent' — 'who is an ardent speaker.'
segments.s661-g86: Two parallel prepositional/adverbial phrases describe manner: به شیرین سخن = 'in/with sweet speech' (به here = 'in' or 'with'), هم = 'also', and به آواز نرم = 'in a soft/gentle voice'. Word order places the manner phrases after the topical clause; the verb ('speaks') is understood from the prior context.
segments.s661-g87: سخن is 'speech/words'. چون here functions temporally/causally 'when/as' and the phrase یک اندر دگر 'one into another' describes joining or interweaving; بافتی is a past-tense poetic verb 'you wove/you joined' (the exact older verbal form and person is archaic/poetic). The clause means 'when you weave speech one into another.'
segments.s661-g88: ازو ('from it' / 'by that') is a prepositional pronoun pointing back to the previous clause, بی‌گمان is an adverb 'undoubtedly', کام دل is a genitive compound 'the heart's desire/fulfillment', and یافتی is a past verb 'you obtained/found'. The sentence states the result: 'from that you will surely obtain the heart's desire.'
segments.s661-g121: بپرسید is an imperative (or polite plural) form 'ask!' (literally 'you ask'); با هر کسی 'with/of any person' marks whom to ask; پیش ازین is the fixed prepositional phrase 'پیش + از + این' = 'before this / previously'. Word order here places the imperative first, then the object phrase 'با هر کسی' and finally the temporal phrase 'پیش ازین'.
segments.s661-g122: سخن راندی is a poetic verbal phrase: سخن (speech/words) + راندی (archaic past form of راندن used here as 'uttered/spoke'); the clause means 'who uttered words'. نامور بیش ازین is a comparative phrase 'more renowned than this' where بیش = 'more' and ازین (از + این) supplies the 'than this' standard.
segments.s661-g123: سبک دارد is a copular expression 'has a light style / his manner is light' (سبک = manner/style; دارد = 'has'). اکنون sets the time 'now'. نگوید سخن is a negated present/persistent statement 'he does not say (any) speech/words' (ن‑ + گوید = 'does not say'); the negated verb follows the temporal phrase and precedes its object.
segments.s661-g124: نه ... نه ... is the correlative 'neither ... nor ...'. Each نه is followed by a prepositional phrase introduced by از ('of/from'): نه از نو = 'neither of the new' (نو = new) and نه از روزگار کهن = 'nor of the ancient times' (روزگار کهن = the old days).
segments.s661-g125: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed narrative phrase 'he gave such an answer' (چنین = thus/so, داد = gave (past), پاسخ = answer). The conjunction/particle که introduces the reported content. گفتار بس is a clipped, idiomatic clause where گفتار = 'speech/talk' and بس = 'enough' (predicate following the noun): 'enough of words.'
segments.s661-g126: به کردار جویم is 'I seek/esteem by deeds' with به marking the manner ('by means of') and جویم = first-person singular present/subjunctive 'I seek/look for'. همه دسترس is a nominal phrase 'all (are) within reach/accessible' where دسترس functions as predicate/adjective ('accessible'); the copula is omitted as typical in Persian.
segments.s661-g127: بپرسید is again the imperative 'ask!'. هنگام شاهان نماز is a genitive/nominal sequence meaning 'the time of the kings' prayers' (هنگام = time/occasion; شاهان = kings; نماز = prayer); Persian often strings nouns together to indicate such relationships.
segments.s661-g128: نبودی is an archaic past negative 'there was not' (نبود + ـی). چنین دراز = 'so/such long' (چنین = such/so; دراز = long). پیش ایشان = 'before/in the presence of them' (پیش = before/in front of; ایشان = them). The clause says that nothing so long existed before them.
segments.s661-g33: ز is the archaic/prepositional form of از (here functioning as 'from'/'by' or introducing amount). بیشی modifies خرد and means 'greater amount' or 'abundance of'; خرد را is the topical/definite object marked by را. بود is the past copula 'was' and links to the predicate سودمند, which is an adjectival predicate 'beneficial/useful'. The phrase literally reads 'From an abundance, wisdom was beneficial.'
segments.s661-g34: همان 'that same' picks out the person just mentioned; بی‌خرد is the adjective/noun 'the unwise/fool'. باشد here is the classical present/subjunctive/copular use (not an imperative) meaning 'is/will be'; اندر is an older preposition 'in/within'; گزند is a noun 'harm/damage'. Together: 'that same fool is (or will be) in harm.'
segments.s661-g35: بگفت‌ش is 'he said to him' (بگفت + enclitic ش = said to him). که introduces the content clause. دانش is the subject 'knowledge'. The construction دانش به از فر شاه is the classical comparative formula 'X به از Y' meaning 'X is better than Y' (به + از together mark the comparison), so here 'knowledge is better than the king's فر (glory)'.
segments.s661-g36: که again introduces an explanatory clause. فر و بزرگی‌ست is a nominal phrase with the copula -ست: 'is glory and greatness' (i.e. 'they are glory and greatness'). زیبای گاه is a genitive/attributive phrase: زیبای = 'beauty/of beauty' (classical ezāfe-like form) and گاه = 'time/occasion', so together 'the beauty (or ornament) of the moment/occasion'. The clause gives a reason: 'for glory and greatness are the beauty of the time.'
segments.s661-g37: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase 'thus gave answer' (چنین + داد + پاسخ). که introduces the reported speech. دانا is the subject 'the wise one'. به فر here is an instrumental/agentive phrase 'by/through (his) فر (glory)', indicating the means by which the action in the next segment occurs.
segments.s661-g38: بگیرد is the present/subjunctive/anticipatory form of گرفتن 'to take/seize' (here rendered 'would take' or 'will take'). جهان 'the world' is the object. سر به سر is an adverbial idiom meaning 'completely / one and all'; each سر is part of that idiom. زیر پر literally 'under (a) feather/wing' is an idiom meaning 'under someone's protection/control' (زیر = under, پر = feather/wing). The whole clause: 'would take the whole world completely under his wing.'
segments.s661-g39: This segment is a coordinated list using و 'and': خرد (wisdom), باید (here 'is necessary' or 'ought' — i.e. 'one needs'), نام (fame/reputation), فر (glory), نژاد (lineage). The structure enumerates the four qualities that ought to be possessed: 'One must have wisdom, name (reputation), glory, and lineage.'
segments.s661-g40: بدین چار is a classical phrase 'by these four' (بدین = به + این, 'by/with these'; چار = 'four'). گیرد is the verb 'takes/seizes' used impersonally of سپهر here; سپهر 'the sky/heavens' is the subject performing the remembrance. از تو یاد is a short genitive-like expression 'of you, remembrance' meaning 'will remember you' or 'will take note of you'. Altogether: 'By these four the heavens will take remembrance of you.'
segments.s661-g209: 'بپرسد' is a third-person narrative present ('he asks' / 'asked') used to introduce an indirect question; 'که' introduces the content clause ('that/why'); the phrase 'گیتی تن‌آسان' places the noun 'گیتی' (the world) before the compound adjective 'تن‌آسان' ('one who makes the body easy' / 'comforts the body'); 'کراست' here is a poetic/copular verbal form linking the predicate (i.e. 'the world makes life easy') — its exact etymology is archaic/poetic, so it functions as the clause's verb. The whole clause reads 'he asks that the world makes life easy for the body.'
segments.s661-g210: 'ز' (poetic 'from/of') introduces the reason or object of feeling ('because of' / 'about'); 'کردار نیکو' = 'good deed(s)' (noun + adjective); 'پشیمان' is a predicate adjective 'repentant/regretful'; 'چراست' is the fused interrogative+copula meaning 'why is it?' — together the clause asks 'why is he regretful about good deeds?'.
segments.s661-g211: 'چنین' = 'thus/so' modifies the following verbal phrase; 'داد' is the past tense 'gave' and 'پاسخ' is its object ('gave an answer'); 'که' introduces the reported content clause; 'یزدان‌پرست' is the subject of that clause ('the God-worshipper / the pious one').
segments.s661-g212: 'بگیرد' is a third-person present/future-like form of گرفتن ('he takes' / 'he will seize'); 'عنان' (reins) is the noun metaphor for 'control'; 'زمانه' is the genitive/possessor of those reins ('of time / of fortune'), the ezāfe is omitted as typical in poetry; 'به‌دست' is an adverbial complement 'into (his) hand' or 'in hand', completing the image 'takes the reins of time into his hand.'
segments.s661-g213: فزونی نجوید is a third-person present (or gnomic) negative: نجوید (does not seek) follows the object فزونی (excess/abundance) — Persian often places the object before the verb. تن‌آسان is a compound adjective meaning “easy-bodied” or “one who lives in ease,” and شود is the intransitive verb “becomes.” Together the clause says: “(one) does not seek excess, (one) becomes peaceful/comfortable.”
segments.s661-g214: چو introduces a temporal/conditional clause (“when/if”). بیشی here means “surplus/excess.” سگالد is an archaic/poetic verbal form meaning “(is) set upon by dogs” or “dogs are set on (it/one)” (agent is not expressed), and هراسان شود is a compound predicate “becomes frightened/terrified.” The whole clause reads: “when surplus is set upon by hounds, (one) is terrified.”
segments.s661-g215: دگر آنک گفتی is a nominal/relative phrase: دگر = “the other/another,” آنک = “that which/which,” and گفتی here refers to what was said (lit. “that which you said”); the following ز کردار نیک specifies the topic “about/from good conduct/deeds.” Note گفتی appears as the referenced utterance (past), so the phrase means “as for the other thing you said about good deeds.”
segments.s661-g216: نهان here functions as ‘hidden’ / an instruction to keep something concealed (elliptical imperative/adjunct); دل و جان are the objects “heart and life/soul.” به بازار نیک gives the locative/figurative place “in the market of good” (i.e., in the sphere/marketplace of virtue). The clause therefore advises keeping one’s heart and life hidden in (or reserved for) the good market.
segments.s661-g1: یکی introduces an indefinite person ('one' / 'a certain'). پیر is an adjectival noun 'old (man)'. بد پهلوانی is a compound adjective meaning 'bad at/poor in heroism' modifying that man. سخن here is a topical/poetic noun 'speech' or 'talk' that connects the description to what follows (it sets up that we are speaking of his words/actions).
segments.s661-g2: به گفتار و کردار is a prepositional phrase 'in (his) speech and deed(s)'. گشته is the perfect/resultant participle/verb 'has become/turned', and کهن is the adjective 'old/ancient' describing the state reached 'has grown old'.
segments.s661-g3: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so'. گوید is the narrative/present reporting verb 'says' (literary present used to introduce a quotation or report). از دفتر پهلوان is a prepositional phrase 'from the hero's book' giving the source.
segments.s661-g4: که introduces reported speech/subordination 'that'. پرسید is the past tense 'asked', with موبد as its subject 'the mobed (Zoroastrian priest)'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from', and نوشین‌روان is the addressee/target 'the sweet‑souled (one)'.
segments.s661-g5: که introduces the embedded question 'what is that'. آن چیست is the interrogative phrase 'what is that' with the older word order placing the copula ست after چی. کز (archaic contraction of که + از) here means 'from' / 'which comes from' and is followed by کردگار جهان 'the Creator of the world'.
segments.s661-g6: بخواهد is the present/future form of خواستن 'to want' and here expresses 'desires/wishes'. پرستنده is the agent 'the worshipper' (the one doing the wanting). اندر نهان is an adverbial phrase 'inwardly/secretly'. Together they form 'which the worshipper secretly wishes'.
segments.s661-g7: بدان is the fused prepositional phrase 'to that' (ب = to/toward + دان/آن 'that'); آرزو is the noun 'desire/wish' and نیز means 'also'. پاسخ دهد is the verbal phrase 'gives an answer' (دهد = gives; literary-present/indicative used in narrative). The clause means 'he also answers that wish.'
segments.s661-g8: بدان پاسخش = 'to that answer' (again the prepositional بِ/ب + اُن/دان idea). بخت فرخ نهد is a compact predicate: بخت فرخ 'fortunate luck/good fortune' and نهد 'sets/places' (third singular verb). Together 'he sets good fortune upon that answer' i.e. 'he blesses that answer with good fortune.'
segments.s661-g297: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed poetic word order: چنين (thus) + داد (past of دادن, 'gave') + پاسخ (answer) — literally 'thus gave (an) answer'. The conjunction که introduces a reported content clause; آز و نیاز are two nouns joined by و (and) serving as the subject/theme of that clause.
segments.s661-g298: سزد‌گر ندارد is an impersonal/elliptical expression meaning 'is not fitting' or 'does not befit' — سزد‌گر (fitting/appropriate) functions as predicate and ندارد (lit. 'does not have') marks negation/existence. خردمند is the target/agent ('the wise'), placed after the predicate in poetic word order. باز is a final poetic particle here (archaic/emphatic); its exact nuance is emphatic and not a standard literal meaning.
segments.s661-g299: تو is the 2nd-person pronoun. از + آز ('from/for greed') expresses cause or characteristic ('given to greed' / 'because of greed'). باشی is a present/subjunctive or optative form of بودن used here in a conditional sense ('if you are'); همیشه is an adverb 'always'; به رنج is a prepositional phrase 'in/unto suffering' indicating result or state.
segments.s661-g300: که introduces the explanatory/result clause ('for/that'). همواره repeats the adverbial sense 'always'. سیری is an abstract noun 'satiety/satisfaction'. نیابی is the negative second-person present/subjunctive (from یافتن) meaning 'you do not attain/find' — here expressing inability to reach satiety. ز (poetic form of از) marks source 'from', and گنج is 'treasure/wealth' as the source.
segments.s661-g301: بپرسید here is a past/perfect or narrative-past form used in epic narration ('they asked' / 'it was asked'); کز is the relative/prepositional clitic 'that/from which' introducing 'which of'. شهریاران is the plural 'kings'. که بیش is a following relative fragment where که introduces the attribute and بیش means 'more/greater' (i.e., 'which is greater').
segments.s661-g302: A series of coordinated prepositional phrases: به هوش 'in/with regard to intelligence', به آیین 'in custom/rite', با رای 'with counsel/advice' and کیش 'creed/sect' completes the list. Repetition of به/با is a stylistic/poetic device to give parallel emphasis to each quality.
segments.s661-g303: Same introductory formula as before: چنین داد پاسخ 'thus gave (an) answer' and که introduces the relative clause describing the king. آن پادشا literally 'that king' (آن functioning as definite demonstrative).
segments.s661-g304: که باشد introduces a relative clause ('who is' or 'who would be'); باشد is the subjunctive/present form of بودن used in relative/descriptive clauses. پرستنده is a participial noun 'worshipper/devotee' and پارسا is an adjective/noun 'pious', joined by و to list two complementary qualities of the king.
segments.s661-g17: بپرسید is a past 3rd-person form meaning “he asked” (used here to introduce reported speech). نیکی کرا is an inverted interrogative phrase: نیکی “goodness” + the archaic enclitic interrogative/relativizer کرا meaning “to whom/for whom”; درخورست is a predicative adjective meaning “is fitting/appropriate,” so the clause means “he asked to whom goodness is fitting” (i.e. who deserves kindness).
segments.s661-g18: به here is the preposition “for/for the,” linking to نام بزرگی (“the name/title of Greatness”); که introduces a relative clause modifying بزرگی; زیباترست is the comparative adjective زیباتر + the copula است fused (poetic spelling) = “is more beautiful.” The whole segment qualifies the name (or title) by saying it is the ‘more beautiful’ greatness.
segments.s661-g19: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed reportive phrase: چنین “thus/so” + داد “gave” + پاسخ “an answer” (past narrative). The conjunction/quotative که introduces the content of the answer; هرکس که begins the conditional/relative clause “whoever (that) …” and گنج is the noun “treasure” that the following adjectives will modify.
segments.s661-g20: بیابد is a 3rd-person singular present/subjunctive form meaning “finds” (here part of the indefinite relative “whoever finds”); پراگنده and نابرده رنج are postpositive modifiers of گنج: پراگنده “scattered,” and نابرده (“not having borne/taken”) + رنج “hardship” together mean “not obtained without toil.” Adjectives/participial phrases commonly follow the noun in classical Persian.
segments.s661-g21: نبخشد is a negative present/optative 3rd-person verb here meaning “he does not give/bestow.” نباشد is a negative subjunctive/optative of بودن meaning “he should not be / is not to be” (classical jussive flavor). سزاوار تخت is a predicate phrase “worthy of the throne,” with سزاوار as adjective and تخت “throne.” Together: “If he does not give, he is not (to be) worthy of the throne.”
segments.s661-g22: زمان تا زمان is an idiomatic repetition meaning “from time to time.” تیره is an adjective “dark/ominous.” گرددش is the verb گردیدن “to turn/become” with the enclitic third‑person object/beneficiary -ش (to him), so تیره گرددش بخت = “his fortune becomes dark.” بخت here means “fortune/luck.”
segments.s661-g23: ز is the poetic preposition “from/of.” هستی و بخشش are a paired noun phrase “existence (being) and giving (generosity).” بود is the past form “was/was made” and مرد مه is a nominal phrase meaning “a great man” (مرد “man” modified by مه used poetically as “great/renowned”). The clause asserts that a noble/great man is constituted by being and generosity.
segments.s661-g24: تو ار گنج داری is a straightforward conditional: تو “you,” ار (if), گنج داری “have treasure/possess wealth.” نبخشی is the 2nd‑person negative present/subjunctive of بخشیدن “you do not give/bestow.” نه به is the start of a continuation: نه is a negation particle “not” and به is the preposition “to/for,” so this fragment means “if you have treasure and do not bestow it (to/for …),” leading into the next line.
segments.s661-g273: چنین (thus) + داد (gave) + پاسخ (answer) is a fixed poetic sequence meaning “gave this answer.” کز is the contracted relative/connector (that/from which) introducing the content of the answer; here it links the preceding clause to the following noun phrase (ارزانیان) as its source/target.
segments.s661-g274: مدارید is a present/imperative-form of the verb used here as an instruction — “be lenient / show forbearance.” باز functions as an adverbial intensifier (here “again/at all” in sense of emphasizing the following negation), ایچ is the archaic negative/indefinite “nothing / not at all,” and سود و زیان are the paired nouns “profit and loss.” Together they form the admonition: ‘be lenient; there is nothing of profit or loss.’
segments.s661-g275: بپرسید is the past-tense narrative verb “asked” (here third-person plural or narrative past). موبد is the addressee/title (the mōbed, Zoroastrian priest). ز is the poetic preposition “from/about,” and کار جهان is the genitive phrase “the affairs/work of the world”; the whole clause means “they asked the mōbed about the world’s affairs.”
segments.s661-g276: سخن برگشاد is a verbal phrase: برگشاد (opened) governs سخن (speech), i.e. “he opened/spoke.” آشکار و نهان are a coordinated adjective pair “manifest (open) and hidden,” describing the content of his speech; word order places the verb before its complements in the poetic line.
segments.s661-g277: که introduces a reported clause (“that…”). آیین is a noun meaning “custom/practice,” and کژ is an adjective “crooked / perverse.” بینم is the 1st-person present form of دیدن (“I see / I find”), used here to report the speaker’s perception; و connects the following adjective ناپسند (“unacceptable / disliked”), so the clause reads ‘that I see the custom crooked and unacceptable.’
segments.s661-g278: دگر here means “other / further,” qualifying گردش کار “the turning/turn of affairs” (گردش = turn, کار = affairs). ناسودمند is an adjective meaning “unprofitable / fruitless,” so the noun phrase gives an additional judgment: ‘the further course/turning of affairs is unprofitable.’ Word order is noun + modifying adjective, typical of Persian poetic phrasing.
segments.s661-g279: چنین داد پاسخ again = “he gave such an answer.” که begins the content clause. زین here is the poetic/contracted form introducing the following noun phrase (literally written as ز + این in other contexts, but in this word-list it is given as زین); چرخ پیر = “this wheel / old wheel” (in Shahnameh usage, the cosmic wheel/fate). The phrase functions as a locative/genitive context for what follows.
segments.s661-g280: اگر هست is the protasis “if there is / if exists.” بادانش here corresponds to با + دانش (poetic orthography) meaning “with knowledge / learned,” and یادگیر is a noun “learner” (one who acquires/retains knowledge). The clause therefore sets a conditional: “if there exists, in this wheel, one who is learned and a learner.”
segments.s661-g377: خردمند کو دل کند بردبار is a compact relative clause: خردمند 'the wise man' + کو functioning as an archaic relative 'who/which' (classical Persian), دل کند is a verbal phrase 'to detach one's heart' (دل + کردن) and کند is the present indicative/ subjunctive verbal form; بردبار is a predicate adjective 'patient/forbearing' that describes the subject. Word order follows classical Persian: subject + relative + verb + predicate adjective.
segments.s661-g378: نباشد به چشم جهاندار خوار is a negative present clause meaning 'is not despised in the eyes of the sovereign.' نباشد is the negative of بودن in this clause (third-person: 'is not' / optative negative), به چشم is an adverbial phrase 'in the sight/eyes of', جهاندار is the agent 'sovereign' (the one whose judgment matters), and خوار is the predicate adjective 'despised/worthless.' The clause states the result/assessment of the preceding relative clause.
segments.s661-g379: بداند که چندست با او هنر uses بداند (third-person present/subjunctive) as an optative/advisory 'let him know' or a statement 'he should know.' که introduces the content clause; چندست ('how much is it') asks for the extent/amount, با او 'with him' indicates possession or accompaniment, and هنر 'skill/ability' is the thing whose extent is in question. The clause reports what ought to be known about the person's skill.
segments.s661-g380: به اندازه یابد ز هر کار بر is a result/continuation clause: به اندازه یابد 'may he find (it) to the proper measure' — به + اندازه marks attainment to a degree, یابد is the verb 'to find/attain,' ز هر کار 'from/for every deed/task' gives the domain, and بر here is a prepositional particle linking the measure to the things compared or judged. Together it prescribes that his skill be realized to the appropriate degree in each action.
segments.s661-g381: گر افزون ازان دوست بستایدش begins with گر 'if' (conditional), افزون ازان 'more than that' (افزون 'more' + ازان 'from/than that'), دوست 'a friend' is the actor, and بستایدش is the verb 'praises him' (third-person imperfect/subjunctive form used in conditional sense). The clause expresses a hypothetical: if praise by a friend goes beyond the proper bounds.
segments.s661-g382: بلندی و کژی بیفزایدش states the consequence: بلندی 'height/loftiness' (figuratively pride/arrogance) and کژی 'crookedness/wrongness' are the things added, and بیفزایدش is the verb 'will add/increase (them) to him.' The coordinated nouns are the effects that excessive praise produces.
segments.s661-g383: همان مرد ایزد ندارد به رنج is a simple clause: همان مرد 'that same man' is the subject, ایزد here denotes divine favor/aid ('God' or 'fortune'), ندارد 'does not have' is the negative present verb, and به رنج 'in suffering' indicates the circumstance in which divine help is lacking. The clause asserts lack of divine support for the man in hardship.
segments.s661-g384: وگر چند گردد پراگنده گنج begins with وگر 'but if' (conditional), چند 'how much/many' here introduces a concessive or quantitative emphasis, گردد 'becomes' is the verb, پراگنده 'scattered' describes the state, and گنج 'treasure/wealth' is the thing dispersed. The clause poses a hypothetical about the dispersion of his wealth (left open or continued in following lines).
segments.s661-g385: پرستش is a noun 'worship' that functions as the object/theme; کند is the third-person singular verb 'does/makes' (here jussive/habitual: 'let him make'); پیشه means 'profession/craft' and is the complement of کند. The conjunction و links this clause to the following noun راستی, which will become the subject of the next verb.
segments.s661-g386: بپیچد is a present-subjunctive/jussive verb meaning 'turn aside' or 'avoid' (3rd person); ز is the poetic preposition 'from/away from'; بی‌راهی is the object 'wrongness/error'; و joins the final noun کاستی 'deficiency/shortcoming'. Together this reads 'let uprightness turn away from error and deficiency.'
segments.s661-g387: بر + ین (an orthographic form of 'این') + برگ + واین (= 'and these') + شاخها form a prepositional phrase 'upon these leaves and these branches'. آخت is a past-form verb meaning 'touched/wrought' or 'applied' (poetic/archaic), and دست 'hand' is the agent; the order (prepositional phrase ... آخت دست) shows poetic inversion with the verb near the end.
segments.s661-g388: هنرمند is the noun 'artisan/artist' modified by the adjective دینی 'religious'; the conjunction و links the appositive/compound epithet یزدان‌پرست 'God‑worshipper/pious one'. Together they describe the owner of the hand mentioned in the previous segment.
segments.s661-g389: همانست is 'همان' ('the same/that') + the copula ست 'is' (3rd sg.), so 'that is'; رای is 'counsel/plan' as predicate. و connects the clause to همینست ('indeed/this is') + راه 'the way/path', repeating the emphatic identification of opinion and path.
segments.s661-g390: به is the preposition 'to/toward'; یزدان is 'God'; گرای here expresses 'incline/turn (toward)' (an imperative/jussive sense in context: 'turn to God'); و links the parallel clause به یزدان پناه where پناه is the noun 'refuge' (elliptically meaning 'seek/take refuge in God').
segments.s661-g391: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. دادگر is the predicate adjective 'just'. باشدی is an archaic 2nd‑person present/subjunctive form 'you be' (here 'if you are' / 'if you should be'); شهریار is the addressed noun 'king' (vocative/explicit subject reference).
segments.s661-g392: ازو is a genitive/agentive pronoun 'of him/you' (here 'of you'); ماند is the verb 'remains/lasts' (3rd singular, here indicating that something will endure); اندر is the preposition 'in/within'; جهان is 'the world'; یادگار is 'a memorial/remembrance'—together: 'a memorial of you will remain in the world.'
segments.s661-g305: ز is the poetic preposition marking source/beneficiary ('to/for' or 'of'); دادار دارنده are two epithets placed together ('the Creator, the Sustainer') in apposition; دارد سپاس literally 'has praise' (دارد from داشتن used statively) so the clause means that praise belongs to or is due to the Creator–Sustainer.
segments.s661-g306: نباشد is the negative optative/existential form ('let there be no' / 'there is no'); کس here, in the scope of that negation, functions as 'anyone/no one' (no one); از رنج او در هراس is a prepositional phrase 'from his suffering in fear' meaning 'afraid because of his suffering'—together: 'let no one be in fear of his suffering.'
segments.s661-g307: پرامید is an adjective 'hopeful' placed before the verb; دارد is the present/stative 'has'; دل نیک‌مرد is a genitive-like noun phrase 'the heart of the virtuous man' (noun+noun order). The clause reads 'the virtuous man has a hopeful heart' (literally: 'hopeful has the heart of the virtuous man').
segments.s661-g308: دل بدکنش را is marked by را as the definite direct object ('the heart of the evildoer'); پر از is the predicative construction 'full of'; بیم و درد 'fear and pain' are the contents. There is no separate overt verb here—the sense 'he fills' or 'he makes ... full' is supplied by the preceding clause's verb or implied by context, so the phrase means '(he) makes the evildoer's heart full of fear and pain.'
segments.s661-g309: سپه را uses را to mark 'the host' as the definite object; بیاراید is a poetic present-tense verb 'he arrays/ador(ns)'; از گنج خویش is a source phrase 'from his treasure' (از = from; خویش = his own, here split as خوی + ش). The clause means 'he arrays the army from his own treasure.'
segments.s661-g310: سوی (toward) precedes the target group; بدسگال 'ill‑fated' qualifies that target; افگند is a past/perfect-form verb 'he cast/threw' (a common epic past form); رنج خویش 'his suffering' is the thing cast. Word order places the direction first: 'toward the ill‑fated he cast his suffering.'
segments.s661-g311: سخن is the object 'a word/question'; پرسد (classical epic form) is a 3rd-person verbal form meaning 'he asked/asks' (Ferdowsi often uses this form for past narrative); از بخردان جهان 'of/from the wise men of the world' is the phrase indicating whom the question is directed to ('he asks the world's sages').
segments.s661-g312: بد و نیک 'bad and good' are a paired noun phrase; دارد is the stative/possession verb 'has' here meaning more idiomatically 'keeps/possesses' (i.e. 'keeps them'); ز دشمن نهان is 'hidden from the enemy' with ز marking 'from' and نهان as the predicate adjective 'hidden.' Together: 'he keeps both bad and good hidden from the enemy.'
segments.s661-g345: بپیچی is a subjunctive/imperative-form verb with the classical prefix ب- plus the present stem (literary instruction) meaning “turn” or “let (it) turn.” دل is the thing affected (theme) — “heart.” از introduces the source/thing to be turned away from, and هرچ نابودنی‌ست is a nominal phrase ‘whatever is perishable’ where نابودنی‌ست is a predicative (is perishable). Word order is verb → theme → prepositional phrase, a common classical poetic ordering for an imperative admonition.
segments.s661-g346: ببخشای is an imperative/subjunctive exhortation from بخشیدن (forgive); here it functions as an address to the reader: “forgive!” آن را is the definite direct object (‘that thing’ + object marker را). که introduces a relative/subordinate clause and بخشودنی‌ست is the predicate ‘is forgivable’ (literal: ‘that which is to be forgiven’). The whole segment gives the parallel imperative: pardon what can be pardoned.
segments.s661-g347: نداری دریغ is an idiomatic negative statement: نداری literally ‘you do not have,’ here functioning as an exhortation meaning ‘have no reluctance / do not begrudge.’ دریغ is the noun ‘reluctance, hesitation.’ آنچه داری ز دوست is a relative phrase ‘that which you have from/for a friend’ (داری = ‘you have,’ ز = poetic ‘from/for’). Together: ‘Do not be reluctant about what you possess for a friend.’
segments.s661-g348: This is a conditional/elliptical extension giving examples of how the friend may desire what you have. اگر introduces the condition (‘if/whether’). دیده here means ‘eye’ (i.e. desire shown by the eye) and خواهد is the present/future sense ‘wants/desires.’ The second اگر repeats the conditional particle for emphasis; مغز و پوست lists extreme parts (‘brain and skin’), an example of hyperbolic desire or cost. Grammatically the second clause is elliptical (the verb from the previous clause is understood).
segments.s661-g349: اگر is the conditional ‘if.’ دوست با دوست literally ‘friend with friend’ expresses reciprocity or ‘one friend against another.’ گیرد is a present-tense verbal form ‘takes/keeps’ (here ‘takes account’ or ‘keeps tally’) and شمار is the object meaning ‘account/number’ (i.e. reckoning). The clause means ‘if one friend makes an accounting against another.’
segments.s661-g350: نباید is an impersonal modal ‘it ought not / there should not be.’ که introduces the subordinate clause and باشد is the present subjunctive ‘that there be’ (existence). می‌انجی is the noun ‘assembly/gathering’ serving as the subject of باشد, and به کار is a prepositional phrase ‘for the matter / for the affair’ (به + کار). Altogether: ‘there should be no assembly needed for the affair,’ i.e. disputes between friends should not be taken to a council.
segments.s661-g351: چو is a temporal/conditional conjunction ‘when/if.’ با مرد بدخواه is a prepositional phrase ‘with a hostile/ill-wishing man.’ باشد is a present/subjunctive copula (‘there is / happens’), and نشست is a noun meaning ‘sitting/meeting’ (a session). The clause thus means ‘when a meeting/sitting occurs with a hostile man’ or ‘when you sit with a hostile person.’
segments.s661-g352: چنان کن is an imperative ‘do thus / act in that manner.’ که introduces a purpose/result clause ‘so that…’ نگشاید is a negative jussive/subjunctive form (classical negative present) meaning ‘may not open / may not be able to [do something]’ — here idiomatically ‘may not lay’ or ‘may not use’ his hand. او is the subject ‘he,’ بر تو is the prepositional phrase ‘against you,’ and دست literally ‘hand’ but idiomatically indicates attack or grasp. The full clause: ‘Act so that he will not be able to put his hand on you.’
segments.s661-g289: بپرسید is a past third-person verb 'asked' (narrative past). کز is the poetic fused form of که + ز and here functions as a relative 'from/which' introducing the source of the pain. The phrase درد بر کیست رنج follows Persian word order where بر + (patient) + است expresses 'upon whom ... is'; کیست is the copula + interrogative 'who is', so the whole unit asks 'from what pain is the suffering?'
segments.s661-g290: که begins a following explanatory clause 'for / because'. سرایست is an archaic fused form (سرای + است) meaning 'is a dwelling/house' (nominal + copula fused into one word). جان را سپنج omits an explicit copula for the second coordinate (common in Persian) and uses را as the object/definiteness marker placed after جان; the structure reads 'the body is like a house and the soul (is) a sponge.'
segments.s661-g291: چنین داد پاسخ is a concise past construction 'thus gave (a) reply' — داد is the past verb 'gave' and پاسخ is its object. که introduces the reported content. این پوده پوست is a nominal phrase 'this patch/piece of skin' (پوده used as 'small woven piece / patch' applied metaphorically to skin).
segments.s661-g292: بود is the past copula 'was.' رنجه here is a noun 'trouble/suffering' predicated of the preceding patch of skin. چندانک is an adverbial 'to that extent / so much that' introducing the consequence clause; مغز اندروست is an inverted small clause meaning 'the brain is in it' — اندروست is an archaic fused expression equivalent to در اوست ('is in it'), hence I mark its printed senses as uncertain.
segments.s661-g293: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. پالود is a perfect/past participle 'purged/removed' (here meaning 'when the soul is taken away'). ز is the poetic preposition 'from', referring back to the body (implicit subject); the subject (تن/body) is omitted and understood from the previous lines. ندارد خرد is a present-tense predicate 'has no wisdom' with ندارد as the verb 'does not have' and خرد 'wisdom'.
segments.s661-g294: که connects an explanatory clause 'that/for'. بر خاک باشد places the (now soulless) body 'upon the earth' — باشد is a present/subjunctive form of 'to be' used narratively ('is/remains'). چو جان بگذرد is another temporal clause 'when the soul passes/departs' with بگذرد as the verb 'passes.' Word order places the locative expression before the verb, a common poetic inversion.
segments.s661-g295: بپرسید again is the past third-person 'they asked'. موبد is the addressee 'the mobed' (Zoroastrian priest). ز پرهیز means 'about/with regard to restraint/abstinence' with ز as the poetic preposition. و گفت follows as 'and he said' — گفت is the past verb 'said' here (the provided sense list only gives noun senses, so that item is marked as not-matching).
segments.s661-g296: که introduces reported speech/indirect question. آز و نیاز are coordinated nouns 'greed and longing/need' (نیاز here means longing/desire). از که is the prepositional question 'from whom'; باید is the modal 'must/ought', and نهفت is used as the verbal/adjectival complement 'be hidden/concealed' — together asking 'from whom must greed and longing be concealed?'.
segments.s661-g353: چو is the conditional 'when/if' (classical/poetic). جوید is a 3rd-person present 'seeks/searches'. The clause has an inverted word order typical of poetry: 'چو جوید کسی راه بایستگی' = 'when/if someone seeks the way of bāyestegi' (راه + noun phrase) with the ezāfe relation implied rather than overt.
segments.s661-g354: باید here is impersonal 'is necessary / is required' and follows the noun هنر; the list 'هنر باید و شرم و شایستگی' is elliptical: 'skill is required, and (so are) modesty and worthiness', so باید governs the whole coordinated noun phrase. و simply joins the items.
segments.s661-g355: نباید is an impersonal negative 'must not / should not'. The phrase 'زبان از هنر چیره‌تر' is a comparative inversion meaning 'the tongue (speech) should not be more dominant/stronger than art/skill' — از introduces the standard of comparison ('than').
segments.s661-g356: This clause is also elliptic/poetic in order: دروغ از هنر نشمرد دادگر = 'A just person (دادگر) should not count/consider (نشمرد) a lie (دروغ) as art/skill (از هنر)'. نشمرد is the negative present 'does not count/consider', and دادگر at the end names the person this applies to ('the just one').
segments.s661-g357: نداند is used in the sense 'does not deem/consider' (rather than simple 'know'). The particle را follows کسی for emphasis/definite object marking in the poetic inversion; 'بزرگی به چیز' means 'greatness in a thing' (به indicates 'in/in terms of'). The overall sense: 'no one considers greatness to be in mere things.'
segments.s661-g358: نه begins the negative continuation 'nor'. خواری ('humiliation') is the noun, taken with به ناچیز ('in/at insignificance'); دارد is the present 'has/holds' and بنیـز (بنیز) is an emphatic 'also/too', so the clause says roughly 'nor does one place humiliation in the insignificant either.'
segments.s661-g359: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. بدگمانی is the subject/cause 'suspicion', and گشاید is the classical/poetic 3rd-person present 'opens/loosens' (transitive), taking زبان ('the tongue' = speech) as its object; word order here mirrors older poetic syntax.
segments.s661-g360: This is an imperative/negative: تو مکن 'do not (you)'; تندی mکن means 'do not be harsh/violent'. هیچ here is an emphatic negative ('at all/never') strengthening مکن. با + بدگمان means 'with the suspicious (person)'; بدگمان functions as a noun/adjective 'one who is suspicious'.
segments.s661-g329: گراینده (a noun) + باشی is an exhortative 2nd-person form (Ferdowsi's optative/imperative-like use of باشی = “be”/“become”). به کردار دین is a prepositional phrase modifying how to be: به + کردار دین = “in the manner/behaviour of religion” (کردار = conduct; دین = the faith). Word order puts the exhortation first and then the descriptive phrase of manner.
segments.s661-g330: بداری is a present-subjunctive/optative use of داشتن (here = “hold/keep”); it continues the injunction started by باشی. بدین is the preposition/prefix ب attached to دین meaning “in/with this” or “by this” and combines with روزگار to yield بدین روزگار = “in these times.” گزین is an imperative/infinitive form meaning “choose” or “select,” placed at the end as the verb of the clause.
segments.s661-g331: خرد را places خرد (“wisdom”) as a definite/direct object (را). کنی is the 2nd-person present/subjunctive of کردن used here as “make” or “set” (i.e., “make wisdom ...”). با دل آموزگار is an instrumental/qualifying phrase: با دل = “with your heart” and آموزگار modifies as the role wisdom should take (“teacher”), so the whole means “make wisdom the teacher of your heart.”
segments.s661-g332: بکوشی is a 2nd-person present-subjunctive/imperative meaning “strive/try.” که introduces the subordinate clause of purpose/result (“so that/that”). نفریبدت is a negative subjunctive form meaning “may it not deceive you” or practically “so that fate does not deceive you”; the final -ت marks the 2nd-person affected (object/recipient). روزگار here names what might deceive you (fate/time).
segments.s661-g333: همان نیز functions as a connective/adverbial (“likewise/also”) linking to the previous advice. یاد گنهکار مرد is a noun phrase where یاد means “memory” or “reputation,” modified by گنهکار مرد (“sinful man”); together it reads “the memory/reputation of a sinful man.” The phrase is topicalized and prepares the content of the following prohibition.
segments.s661-g334: نباشی is the negative 2nd-person imperative/subjunctive (“do not be” / “let it not be [for you]”) giving the prohibition. به بازار ننگ و نبرد is a locative/figurative phrase: به بازار = “in the market (i.e. public place/reputation),” ننگ و نبرد = “shame and battle/strife” specifying the disreputable arena; together: “do not be (remembered) in the market of shame and strife.”
segments.s661-g335: غم آن جهان literally = “sorrow for that world” (آن جهان = the other/after world). از پی این جهان strings the temporal/teleological relation: از پی = “following/after,” این جهان = “this world”; together the phrase expresses ‘sorrow for the other world that comes after this one.’ Word order places the object of feeling (غم آن جهان) before the explanatory temporal phrase.
segments.s661-g336: نباید is an impersonal modal meaning “one must not/you should not.” که introduces the clause that follows. داری is the 2nd‑person present of داشتن “to have/hold” meaning here “harbor” or “keep.” به دل در نهان is a locative/qualifying phrase: به دل = “in (your) heart,” در نهان = “secretly/hidden”; together they specify where one must not keep that sorrow.
segments.s661-g361: اینِ ترکیبِ پیوندی «ازان پس چو ... برد» با «ازان پس» به معنی «بعد از آن» شروع می‌شود. «چو» به معنی «وقتی» است و جملهٔ وابستهٔ زمانی می‌سازد؛ «سستی گمانی» ترکیبِ اسمِ مرکب است (سستی = سستی/رُخوتِ ذهنی، گمانی = ظنّ یا پنداشت)، و «برد» فعل سوم شخص مفرد است که در متن کهن به معنی «می‌گیرد/حاکم می‌شود/پدید می‌آید» به کار رفته است؛ پس ساختار یعنی «بعد از آن وقتی که سستیِ گمان پدید آید/حاکم شود».
segments.s661-g362: «وز اندازه گفتار او بگذرد» بخشِ نتیجه/گسترشِ معناییِ جمله است: «وز» در این موقعیت متنی نشانهٔ پیوندیِ شاعرانۀ «از/و از» است، «اندازه گفتار» = حدّ و اندازهٔ سخن، «او» ضمیر فاعلیِ سوم شخص است، و «بگذرد» در اینجا به معنی «فراتر رود/از حد بگذرد» است؛ کلّاً یعنی سخنش از حدّ متعارف می‌گذرد.
segments.s661-g363: جملهٔ امری/دستوری به مخاطب است: «تو» ناظر، «پاسخ» = پاسخ/پاسخ‌گویی، «مر» = «مرا» (قدیمی)، و «او را» مفعولی که مربوط به «پاسخ» است (یعنی پاسخِ او را). «به اندازه گوی» عبارتِ قیدی است که «به اندازه» = «در حدِّ مناسب»، و «گوی» در مقام فعل امری به معنی «بگو/گوی» آمده است؛ پس: «تو، پاسخِ او را به اندازه بگو (برای من)».
segments.s661-g364: این بند فقرهٔ دیگری از دستورهاست: «سخن‌های چرب آور» صفت و مفعول مرکّب است که به معنی «کلامِ چرب‌وحلقوم = مدح‌آمیز/چرب» است و «آور» به معنی «بیاور/حامل باش» فعل یا وصف فاعلی است. «و تازه گوی» قیدِ دیگری است که «تازه» = تازه/نو می‌گو و «گوی» دوباره فعل امری «بگو» را تکیه می‌دهد؛ در مجموع: «سخنان چاپلوسانه بیاور و تازه (نو) بگو».
segments.s661-g365: «به آزرم اگر بفگنی سوی خویش» ساختار شرطی/شرط‌گونه دارد: «به» حرفِ اضافه به معنی «به/نسبت به/برای»، «آزرم» در اینجا نامِ شخص یا واژه‌ای خاص است (نامِ خاص)، «اگر» شرطی می‌آورد، «بفگنی» شکلِ کهنِ فعلی است به معنی «بگویی/افشا کنی»، «سویِ خویش» = «نسبت به خودت/دربارهٔ خودت» (سوی = به طرف)، و «-ش» ضمیر ملکی متصل است؛ یعنی «اگر به آزرم دربارهٔ خودت بگویی».
segments.s661-g366: «پشیمانی آید به فرجام پیش» جملهٔ خبری است: «پشیمانی» = حسرت/ندامت، «آید» فعل مضارع/صحراوی به معنی «می‌آید/پدید می‌آید»، «به فرجام» = «در پایان/در نتیجه»، و «پیش» = «پیش/پیش از آن» (اینجا دلالتِ زمانی یا معنای متمم دارد). مجموعاً: «ندامت/پشیمانی پیش از فرجام وارد می‌شود/پدید می‌آید».
segments.s661-g367: «چو بیکار باشی مشو رامشی» جملهٔ شرطی/زمانی‌ست: «چو» = «وقتی/هرگاه»، «بیکار باشی» = «تو بیکار باشی/وقتی تو بیکار هستی» (باشی شکل قدیمیِ وجهِ مضارع یا التزامی برای «باشی/هستی»)، «مشو» منفیِ امری به معنی «مَشو/مَنباش/محوّل مشو» یعنی «میشو مباش»، و «رامشی» = آرامش/رامیّت/تسلیم‌پذیری؛ بنابراین «هرگاه بیکار شدی، رامش/تسلیم‌پذیری مگذار/میشو».
segments.s661-g368: «نه کارست بیکاری ار باهشی» جملهٔ توضیحی است: «نه کارست بیکاری» = «بیکاری کار نیست/بیکاری به شمارِ کار نمی‌آید» (کارست به صورت کهن، به معنی «کار/اشتغال» یا «چیزی که کار است» به کار رفته)، «ار» = «اگر»، و «باهشی» = «با او باشی/چنان که همراه او باشی» (حرف اضافهٔ همراه و ضمیر متصل). پس: «بیکاری کار نیست، اگر با او باشی».
segments.s661-g337: نشستنت is the verb نشستن nominalized with the enclitic -ت ("your sitting" or "your company"). همواره is an adverb ("always") that modifies the nominalized verb, and با + بخردان is a prepositional phrase ("with the wise ones"); Persian places the adverb before the prepositional phrase here, so the whole segment reads literally as "your always sitting with the wise."
segments.s661-g338: گراینده is an agent/participial noun ("that which causes / one who causes to incline") used predicatively; it governs the object رامش ("tranquility"). جاودان is a postpositive adjective modifying رامش ( Persian adjectives commonly follow the noun), so the phrase means "causing (or leading to) eternal tranquility." The copula is implied.
segments.s661-g339: گراینده again is a participial/agentive noun ("a guider / that which inclines"). بادی here names a windlike force ("a breeze / wind") modifying or appositive to گراینده, so "a guiding breeze." به introduces the goal/direction ("toward") and فرهنگ و رای lists two aims joined by و ("learning/culture and counsel/opinion"). Word order: agent + specifying noun + prepositional goal.
segments.s661-g340: به here marks the relation 'for/to' (direction or beneficiary). یزدان modifies خرد to yield a compound sense like "divine wisdom" (literally "Godly/divine wisdom"). بایدت is باید plus the enclitic -ت (= "it is necessary for you / you must"); Persian often puts باید + object after it, so رهنمای ("a guide") is the predicate nominal meaning "a guide is necessary for you."
segments.s661-g341: از اندازه بر functions as an adverbial phrase meaning "beyond measure/degree." نگذرانی is a negative jussive/optative form (ن‑ prefix + verb) addressed to the second person, i.e. "do not let ... pass/occur." سخن is the direct object ("speech/words"). Literal order: "From measure beyond do not cause speech to pass."
segments.s661-g342: که introduces an explanatory clause ("for / because / that"). تو is the 2nd‑person pronoun; نو is an adjective "new" ("inexperienced"). به + کاری expresses being "in/at a task or undertaking" (به here = "in/at/to"). گیتی کهن is a noun + adjective sequence where گیتی ("world") is followed by کهن ("ancient"); together the clause says you are new at a task in an ancient world. Note: کاری in this text is the noun "a work/task" (not the verbal senses listed in the lemma file).
segments.s661-g343: نگرداندت is a verb form with the 2nd‑person enclitic -ت and a negative/optative sense here ("may it not make you / may it not cause you to become"); it governs the following terms as what should not be brought about. رامش is one object ("tranquility"). و links to the next phrase رود مست: رود here functions as a subjunctive/optative of رفتن ("to go/become") and مست modifies it ("drunk"), so together the coordinate means "and may you not go/become drunk." (Some lexical lists treat رود as a noun, but here it is verbal/optative.)
segments.s661-g344: نباشدت is the optative/negative form (نباشد) with the 2nd‑person enclitic -ت: "may you not be." با مردم بد نشست is a prepositional phrase plus noun phrase: با مردم ("with people") modified by بد ("bad/evil") and نشست is a noun meaning "sitting/association/meeting" (here: "company/companionship"). Together: "may you not be in sitting/association with bad people."
segments.s661-g369: "ز هرکار کردن" is a prepositional/verbal-noun phrase (z + infinitive) meaning "in/for doing any work." "تو را" uses the object/experiencer marker را attached to the pronoun to indicate who is affected, and the predicate "ننگ نیست" is an existential negative: literally "shame is not (for you)." Word order is typical poetic Persian: prepositional phrase + experiencer + existential predicate.
segments.s661-g370: Here "اگر چند" functions concessively as "although/even if." The doubled preposition "با ... و با ..." marks two parallel qualities: "with fragrance and with color/appearance." The final "نیست" negates the clause: "it is not with scent nor with color." The clause qualifies the preceding statement (concessive condition).
segments.s661-g371: "به نیکی" is an adverbial phrase meaning "with goodness" or "doing well." "به هر کار" specifies the domain "in every task/deed," and the predicate "کوشا بود" is the past copula + adjective meaning "was diligent/striving." Note the past copula "بود" gives a simple past/state reading.
segments.s661-g372: "همیشه" is the adverb "always." "به دانش" literally "toward knowledge" functions as a purposive/goal phrase meaning "for knowledge" or "in pursuit of learning." "نیوشا بود" is an archaic/poetic predicate: here it describes an eager or listening attitude toward learning ("was keen/eager for knowledge"). The copula "بود" again gives past/state. (The headword "نیوشا" in the supplied senses does not match this archaic adjectival use, so see senses mapping.)
segments.s661-g373: "به کاری نیازد" uses "نیازد" (from نیازیدن) in a prescriptive/advisory sense here: "one should not undertake/need to do a certain work." The relative particle "که" introduces the clause describing that work: "فرجام اوی" = "its outcome/end." The possessive pronoun "اوی" refers back to "کاری."
segments.s661-g374: Objects "پشیمانی و تندی" ("regret and sharpness/anger") precede the verb "آرد" (third-person singular, poetic present/future sense "brings/will bring"). "به روی" literally "to the face" means "appear on the face" or "affect one outwardly." Together: "whose end brings regret and harshness to the face (i.e. causes sorrow and anger)."
segments.s661-g375: "ببخشاید" is a jussive/optative verb form meaning "may he spare/let him grant/spare." The phrase "از درد بر مستمند" places the thing spared after از ("from suffering") and the beneficiary after بر ("to/on/for the needy"); taken together it reads "spare the needy from suffering."
segments.s661-g376: In this clause "نیارد" is an archaic poetic negative/optative form (functionally close to "نیاورد" / "may not bring" or "let it not cause"). "دلش" = "his heart" (دل + enclitic "ش" = his), "سوی" = "toward," and "درد و گزند" = "pain and harm." The whole means "may his heart not turn toward pain and harm." (The supplied lemma/senses for "نیارد" do not match this reading, so sense is marked unavailable.)
segments.s661-g321: هراس تو آنگه که جویی گزند is a nominal clause followed by a subordinate clause introduced by آنگه که. هراس تو = “your fear” (noun + post‑posed personal pronoun). آنگه که introduces a temporal/conditional subordinate clause “when/that …”; جویی is the 2nd‑person present/subjunctive form of the verb 'seek' (classical Persian uses ی‑final forms after particles like که/آنگه), and گزند is the object ‘harm’. Word order is literally “fear (your) — then that you seek harm.”
segments.s661-g322: وزو ایمنی چون بود سودمند links back to the previous clause: وزو (here 'of it / from that' in an older/poetic form) indicates source; ایمنی = 'safety' (noun). چون functions as 'how' (question/retorical 'how would...'), بود is the past form of 'to be' used here with a conditional/result sense ('would be'/'was'), and سودمند = 'useful/beneficial'. The phrase asks rhetorically how safety, given that fact, could be of use.
segments.s661-g323: وگر نیک‌دل باشی و راه‌جوی is a conditional protasis introduced by وگر ('if'). نیک‌دل is an adjective 'kind‑hearted'; باشی is the dependent/subjunctive 2nd‑person form of 'to be' used after the conditional particle (classical Persian uses ی‑ending subjunctive). و links the two predicates, and راه‌جوی is a predicative noun 'seeker of the path' (used in apposition / as the second predicate after باشی).
segments.s661-g324: بود نزد هر کس تو را آبروی is the apodosis/result clause. بود ('was'/'would be') serves as the main verb (here with prospective/conditional sense). نزد هر کس = 'before/in the sight of every person' (prepositional phrase). تو را آبروی = 'your reputation' with را marking the definite object; word order is inverted for poetic effect (verb and prepositional phrase precede the object).
segments.s661-g325: وگر بدکنش باشی و بد تنه begins the conditional 'if' (وگر). بدکنش is a compound meaning roughly 'evil‑doer' or 'one who does bad deeds' used predicatively; باشی is the subjunctive/dependent form of 'to be' after the conditional. و links a second predicate بد تنه, an archaic/poetic phrase meaning 'of bad nature/character' (تنه here is figurative). The segment sets up the negative condition.
segments.s661-g326: به دوزخ فرستاده باشی بنه is the result clause: به + دوزخ = 'into/to hell'; فرستاده is the past participle 'sent'; باشی again is the dependent/subjunctive/copular form producing the conditional/result meaning 'would be sent'. بنه is an obscure/poetic particle here (not the pistachio sense) and functions as an emphatic or archaic closing particle — its exact lexical sense is not one of the listed senses.
segments.s661-g327: مباش ایچ گستاخ با این جهان is an admonition. مباش is the prohibitive (do not be) form of 'to be'; ایچ is an intensifier meaning 'at all'/'nothing'; گستاخ = 'insolent/impudent'; با این جهان = 'toward/with this world'. Together: 'Do not be at all insolent toward this world.'
segments.s661-g328: که او راز خویش از تو دارد نهان is a causal/explanatory clause introduced by که ('for/because'). او = 'it/he' (here 'the world'); راز خویش = 'its own secret' (خویش is formed by خوی + ش, with ش the possessive enclitic); از تو = 'from you'; دارد نهان = 'keeps (it) hidden' (دارد + adjective نهان, literally 'has/keeps hidden').
segments.s661-g313: بپرسید here is narrative past ('they asked') introducing an indirect question. کار پرستش is a noun phrase where کار 'affair/deed' is qualified by پرستش 'worship' (genitive/attributive relationship). به چیست is the question phrase: the preposition به + interrogative چی 'what' + the copula ست 'is' yields 'what is (the purpose/nature) of...'.
segments.s661-g314: به نیکی is a prepositional phrase 'toward/to goodness' (به marks direction or aim). یزدان گراینده is a noun phrase built with the agent noun/participle گراینده 'one who inclines/causes to turn' tied to یزدان 'God' (i.e. 'whom God turns/whom God inclines'). کیست ('who is it?') is the interrogative copular clause closing the question.
segments.s661-g315: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed narrative phrase: چنین 'thus' + داد 'gave' (past) + پاسخ 'answer' — 'he gave such an answer'. که introduces the reported content ('that/that which'). تاریک‌خوی is the predicate adjective/nominal phrase 'dark‑dispositioned, sullen' and serves as the subject/property described in the reported clause.
segments.s661-g316: روان اندر آرد is a verbal clause with روان 'the soul/spirit' as subject and آرد (from آوردن) 'brings/causes to come' as the verb; اندر is the preposition 'into/in' that combines with آرد to mean 'brings into'. به باریک موی is the result phrase 'to/into a narrow/fine hair' (a poetic idiom meaning 'to a hair's breadth'); باریک modifies موی in inverted, poetic order.
segments.s661-g317: نخست is a discourse adverb 'first'. آنک introduces a relative clause 'he who/that which' + داند 'knows' (present/past classical form) + که هست 'what/is that which exists' (که here = 'what/that'). و یکی‌ست ties on with 'and he is one' (یکی‌ست = 'is one' — an emphatic, somewhat archaic copular phrase asserting unity).
segments.s661-g318: ترا is the beneficiary/object 'to you' formed by the second‑person clitic + the object marker (ت + را). زین نشان is a poetic contraction of ز این نشان 'from this sign' (زین = ز + این), giving a prepositional phrase of source. رهنمای اندکی‌ست is the predicate: رهنمای 'guide' + اندکی‌ست 'is small/of little value' — literally 'the guide is small', i.e. 'gives little guidance'.
segments.s661-g319: ازو دارد is a source‑first construction: ازو 'from him' (source) + دارد 'has/receives'. از کار نیکی سپاس is the remainder 'praise from/of the good deed' where از marks origin ('because of/from the deed of goodness') and سپاس 'praise/thanks' is the object. Together: 'he receives from him praise on account of the good deed.'
segments.s661-g320: بدو باشد ایمن is a short clause 'to him (بدو) he is/should be (باشد) safe (ایمن)' where بدو marks the beneficiary/target. و introduces the contrasting clause. زو در هراس is 'and from him (زو, poetic for از او) in fear (در هراس)' — a compact, classical way to say one is both secure with him and yet in awe/fear of him. Note the poetic contractions: زو = ز + او (from him).
